Saturday dinner-only (19:00-22:00) is unusual; verify if planning a Saturday lunch. Reservation window opens the 1st of the prior month and phone booking is required for any dietary restrictions. GF confidence is rated 'ask' but celiac diners should treat this as effectively 'no' given wheat-pasta kitchen with no structural accommodation at lunch and only limited at dinner.

lead: トラットリア トゥッティ
name_ja: トラットリア トゥッティ
cuisine: Italian / Pasta
address_ja: 長野県長野市鶴賀権堂町2334 サンセットプラザ 103
geocode_note: Matched to 長野県長野市大字鶴賀権堂町２３３４番地 via GSI address search; coordinates fall within target bounding box and approximately 792m from Zenkō-ji, well within the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 権堂町 (Gondō-chō), 権堂駅より徒歩約6分
seats_text: 3 counter seats at the kitchen bar plus 4 table seats; extremely intimate solo-run space
closed_days: 木曜日 (Thursday); additional irregular closures
cultural_comfort_note: Solo proprietor, tiny 7-seat space on a back alley; no English signage or menu noted, though standard Italian dish names are internationally recognizable — best suited for diners comfortable with Japanese-only interaction.
vegan_detail: No vegan menu or plant-based options advertised. Standard Italian trattoria repertoire includes anchovies, prosciutto, bacon, cheese, eggs, and butter throughout — appetizers, pasta sauces, and desserts all involve animal products. Hidden anchovy paste has been noted in appetizers. Vegan accommodation would require direct inquiry and may not be feasible given the fixed small-batch daily menu run by a single cook.
gf_detail: All pasta dishes reviewed are conventional wheat-based pasta; no gluten-free pasta alternatives have been mentioned in any source. The kitchen is a small open counter where wheat pasta is the core product. No dedicated fryer information available. No gluten-free claims or accommodations found anywhere. Standard Italian wheat-based soy sauce is not typically used in Italian cooking, but pasta, bread accompaniments, and some sauces will contain wheat. Diners with celiac disease should call ahead — accommodation is uncertain and cross-contamination risk is likely given the compact open kitchen.

bio.background: Trattoria Tutti opened on August 8, 2017, tucked into a back alley off the path between Gondō Station and Zenkō-ji Temple, occupying a compact unit in Sunset Plaza. The proprietor runs the entire operation solo — cooking, plating, and serving — in a space with just seven seats.
bio.specialty: Rotating seasonal pasta: dishes like amatriciana with crispy pancetta, broccoli and anchovy linguine, prosciutto with local black oyster mushrooms from Nakano, and peperoncino with bottarga have all appeared on the menu alongside house-made gelato in up to seven seasonal fruit varieties.
bio.philosophy: The kitchen draws on Nagano's local seasonal produce — mushrooms, apples, local herbs — married to Italian technique and premium tableware (Richard Ginori, Wedgwood, Riedel), signaling that the proprietor treats the full dining ritual, not just the food, as the offering.

anecdotes.text: A diner came specifically after hearing from a friend that the gelato was exceptional — 'heard from an acquaintance about a place with outstanding gelato' — and found all seven seasonal fruit varieties genuinely delicious.
One reviewer described the proprietor as 'skillfully balancing cooking and service solo, creating an atmosphere where wine and dishes complemented one another perfectly.'

caveats: No official website found. Hours from Tabelog (12:00-14:30 / 18:00-21:30) differ slightly from other sources (11:30-14:00 / 17:30-21:30); Tabelog is treated as authoritative but the restaurant notes irregular closures — call ahead. Cash only confirmed (no cards, no e-money). No GF or vegan accommodations advertised anywhere; safety confidence is none as no allergy-handling information exists in any source. The proprietor runs solo so the tiny menu changes daily and advance communication about dietary needs is essential.

cuisine: 焼肉・ジンギスカン (Yakiniku / Jingisukan)
address_ja: 長野県長野市鶴賀権堂町2276
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch for 長野市大字鶴賀権堂町２２７６; coordinates within ~985m of Zenkoji target center, well within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 権堂町 (Gondo), 権堂駅より徒歩約1分
seats_text: 50 seats total across counter, table, and semi-private sections
hours_raw: 月〜土 17:00〜24:00（LO 23:30）、日曜定休（祝日も休み、但しGW等は営業の場合あり）
closed_days: 日曜・祝日（GW等例外あり）
cultural_comfort_note: A casual neighbourhood grilled-meat spot with no English menu or signage found; basic Japanese or pointing at a menu will get you seated and fed.
vegan_detail: The entire menu is centred on lamb, pork offal, and horsemeat. No plant-based dishes, tofu, or vegetable-only options are listed anywhere. Not suitable for vegans or vegetarians.
gf_detail: Jingisukan and yakiniku tare (dipping/marinating sauces) typically contain wheat-based soy sauce, and no gluten-free variants are mentioned. The signature "Jinhoru nabe" finishing course adds Hokkaido ramen noodles to the pot — a direct gluten source at the table. No dedicated GF menu or allergen sheet is documented. People avoiding gluten should inquire carefully before ordering any sauce or broth item.

bio.background: Opened in February 2014 in Nagano's lively Gondo entertainment district by an owner from Hokkaido, bringing northern Japan's grilled-lamb tradition south to the landlocked city.
bio.origin: Hokkaido, Japan
bio.specialty: ジンホル鍋 (Jinhoru Nabe) — a communal hot-pot combining Hokkaido-seasoned lamb (jingisukan) and miso-flavoured pork offal at ¥1,180, finished tableside with Nishiyama ramen noodles dunked into the rich meat broth
bio.philosophy: Authentic Hokkaido-style preparation using direct-sourced ingredients: the lamb and offal come from Hokkaido suppliers, and seasonal bafun-uni (sea urchin) is shipped straight from Hokkaido fishers — a rare detour from the core grill menu.

anecdotes.text: The finishing move — dropping Nishiyama ramen into the Jinhoru Nabe broth and eating it as a dipping noodle — is described on multiple listing pages as 'the connoisseur's way' to end the meal.
Fresh bafun-uni (sea urchin) delivered directly from Hokkaido fishermen is offered at ¥1,800 as a premium side — unusual for a grilled-meat restaurant.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Hokkaido ramen noodles (wheat) are cooked tableside in the shared Jinhoru Nabe pot as the standard finishing course, creating direct gluten contact with the broth served to everyone at the table.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Yakiniku and jingisukan tare sauces in Japan standardly contain wheat-based soy sauce; no gluten-free sauce alternative is mentioned anywhere in the restaurant's listings.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The restaurant serves only meat and offal; there is no plant-based cooking surface or equipment — full cross-contact with animal products throughout.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, allergy-handling policy, or staff training for dietary restrictions is mentioned in any available source. Phone enquiry (026-233-7357) before visiting is the only documented route for special requests.

caveats: Payment: HotPepper (venue-managed) states cash and PayPay only — no credit cards or electronic money — contradicting older Tabelog data that listed Visa/Master/AMEX; the HotPepper data is treated as more current. Hours on HotPepper extend to 24:00 vs. 23:30 last order confirmed on both sources. Holiday schedule (open GW, closed other holidays) should be confirmed by phone. No official standalone website found; nc3v501.gorp.jp is a listing-aggregator page used as the closest proxy.

cuisine: Cafe / Wine Bar
address_ja: 長野県長野市権堂町2373
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI address search for 長野県長野市権堂町2373; coordinates fall within the target bounding box and approximately 1.58km from Zenko-ji, just within the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 権堂町
seats_text: 16 seats; mix of counter and table seating; solo dining explicitly welcomed
hours_raw: 11:30〜22:00（金・土・祝前日は24:00まで）、月曜定休＋不定休あり
closed_days: 月曜、不定休あり
cultural_comfort_note: A relaxed, welcoming counter-and-table cafe-bar where solo visitors are explicitly encouraged; no English menu confirmed but the friendly neighborhood atmosphere and credit-card acceptance make it accessible.
vegan_detail: No vegan menu labeling found. The food menu skews European-style small plates — Basque cheesecake (dairy), pork pâté, open sandwiches, chocolate terrine — with no plant-based labeling. Some items (fruit marinade, coffee, wine) are incidentally vegan, but hidden dairy and meat are the norm. Ask before ordering.
gf_detail: No gluten-free labeling or dedicated GF options confirmed. The kitchen serves sandwiches and pastry-based desserts, so wheat cross-contact is a real concern. Soy sauce use is unconfirmed but likely in any savory items. No dedicated fryer information available. Guests with celiac disease should inquire directly and proceed with caution.

bio.background: The owner is a Gondo district native who built restaurant experience in Tokyo and Hiroshima before returning to open this spot in December 2022, motivated by a desire to revitalize the Gondo shopping arcade.
bio.specialty: Basque cheesecake — lightly salted, low-sugar, and designed to pair with both coffee and wine — is the signature draw, alongside hand-drip coffee sourced from Tokyo roaster OBSCURE COFFEE ROASTER.
bio.philosophy: Equal parts daytime coffee sanctuary and evening wine bar, conceived so that drinkers and non-drinkers alike can drop in without ceremony. The name nods to the beckoning-cat (招き猫) tradition.
bio.origin: 権堂町, Nagano City

anecdotes.text: The owner spent years working in restaurants in Tokyo and Hiroshima before coming home to Gondo, the neighbourhood he grew up in, to open MIKENEKO STAND in December 2022.
The Basque cheesecake is deliberately low in sugar and finished with a light salt, engineered to complement a glass of wine as much as an afternoon coffee.

caveats: Hours extend to 24:00 on Fri/Sat/holiday eves per Web-Komachi; irregular closure days are not pre-announced so check Instagram (@mikeneko_stand) or X (@MIKENEKOSTAND) before visiting. No dietary allergy information is published anywhere — all GF/vegan safety assessments require direct staff inquiry. Seat count (16) from Web-Komachi; Retty lists operating status as unknown but multiple 2024-era blog posts confirm it is operating.

name_ja: リストランテ カンパネッラ
cuisine: Italian-French fusion with Japanese accents; seasonal vegetables grown at the chef's own farm in Takayama Village
neighborhood: 大門町 / Daimon-cho, inside Patio Daimon complex; approx. 6 min walk from Gondo Station
address_ja: 長野県長野市大字長野大門町54-1 ぱてぃお大門 蔵楽庭内
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch returned [138.187943, 36.656933]; approx. 0.5 km from Zenkoji — well within the 1.61 km target radius.
seats_text: 32 seats; terrace seating available April–November (pet-friendly)
hours_raw: Mon–Fri lunch 11:30–14:00, dinner 17:30–22:00; Sat–Sun–holidays lunch 11:30–14:30, dinner 17:30–21:00; closed Wednesdays and the 3rd Tuesday of each month
closed_days: 水曜日、毎月第3火曜日 (Wednesdays and the 3rd Tuesday of each month)
cultural_comfort_note: Standard Japanese dining environment with no English-language signage or menu confirmed; located in a tourist-adjacent complex near Zenkoji so some familiarity with visitors is likely, but communicating dietary needs will require Japanese or advance contact.
vegan_detail: Menu centres on Italian-French courses featuring pasta, seafood, meat, and house-made desserts. No vegan labelling found on any source. The chef's farm supplies most vegetables, meaning produce-forward dishes exist, but hidden animal products — butter, cream, dashi, cheese — are standard in this cuisine style. Any vegan need would require advance notice and direct confirmation with the kitchen.
gf_detail: Pasta is a signature item, meaning wheat is central to the menu. No gluten-free labelling or dedicated GF options appear on any source reviewed. Italian-French sauces may use wheat-based thickeners or flour. Japanese soy sauce (含む小麦) is plausible in fusion-inflected dishes. No mention of a dedicated fryer or allergen protocols. Celiac diners should contact the restaurant well in advance — do not assume any dish is safe without direct kitchen confirmation.
caveats: No official website beyond the Patio Daimon tenant page. Hours cross-checked across Tabelog, HotPepper, and deli-koma; lunch open time shown as 11:00 on some aggregators and 11:30 on others — 11:30 used as the more conservative figure from the official tenant page. Third-Tuesday closure is confirmed on multiple sources. Chef name not publicly disclosed on any source found.

bio.background: Ristorante Campanella occupies a converted traditional kura (warehouse) storehouse within the Patio Daimon complex, a curated cluster of historic earthen-walled buildings just steps from Zenkoji Temple's main approach. The restaurant blends Italian and French technique with Japanese seasonal sensibility.
bio.specialty: House-made pasta courses featuring vegetables grown at the chef's own field in Takayama Village, complemented by locally sourced fish and carefully selected meats; all dishes from antipasto through dessert are prepared entirely in-house.
bio.philosophy: Time-consuming preparation enhances the perfection of the dishes — the kitchen sources most vegetables from the chef's personal farm in the mountain village of Takayama, expressing the seasons directly on the plate.
bio.origin: Nagano City, operating within the Patio Daimon heritage complex in the Daimon-cho district

anecdotes.text: A loyal regular notes visiting approximately 15 times a year and calls it undoubtedly the most delicious restaurant in Nagano.
One diner discovered the restaurant while attending Zenkoji's rare once-every-seven-years special opening, the Gokaicho — a chance encounter that left a lasting impression.
The young chef cultivates his own vegetable garden to supply the kitchen with fresh produce, a hands-on commitment to farm-to-table that reviewers notice in the quality of seasonal dishes.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Pasta is a menu centrepiece; wheat flour is in active use throughout the kitchen. No information about cross-contamination controls was found on any source.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: The menu features Japanese-inflected Italian-French dishes. Standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat; use in sauces or marinades cannot be ruled out without direct inquiry.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Butter, cream, cheese, and other dairy are standard in Italian-French cuisine; shared preparation surfaces and cookware are expected. No vegan-separated prep protocols are mentioned anywhere.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen menu, staff allergy training, or dietary accommodation protocol is mentioned on any reviewed source. Advance phone contact (026-233-4560) or email (campanella.if@nifty.com) is the only available channel for dietary queries.

positives.text: The kitchen grows most vegetables at the chef's own farm in Takayama Village, meaning produce quality and provenance are unusually transparent for an Italian restaurant in this region.
All dishes from antipasto through dessert are entirely house-made, giving the kitchen full control over ingredients — a meaningful advantage for custom dietary requests made well in advance.

cuisine: Italian (Tuscan-style)
address_ja: 長野県長野市桜枝町901
geocode_note: Coordinates from geocoding.jp for 長野県長野市桜枝町901. GSI returned only a broader district-level match (長野市桜, lng 138.1526 outside target box); geocoding.jp returned lat 36.65855 / lng 138.184604, which falls within the target box and places the restaurant approximately 390m from Zenkoji — consistent with Tabelog's stated ~5-minute walk.
neighborhood: 桜枝町 (Sakuraeda-machi), 善光寺門前エリア
seats_text: Small intimate space; one source notes 6 tables only; total seating approximately 24 per deli-koma. No private rooms.
closed_days: 月曜日定休。日曜はランチのみ（ディナーなし）。
cultural_comfort_note: A small neighborhood Italian run by a Japan-trained owner with no evidence of English menus or English-speaking staff; basic Japanese will be needed to navigate the menu and communicate dietary needs.
vegan_detail: The menu centers on pasta, pizza, seafood, and meat dishes — all standard Italian preparations with no advertised vegan options. Butter, cheese, and anchovies appear throughout. Vegans would need to inquire in Japanese about ingredient substitutions; options are likely very limited.
gf_detail: Pasta and pizza are core menu items and both rely heavily on wheat flour. No gluten-free pasta or flour alternatives are advertised anywhere. Cross-contamination risk from pasta cooking water is high in a small open kitchen. Celiac diners should call ahead in Japanese (026-235-6512) before visiting; safe choices are unlikely without advance coordination.

bio.background: A small, owner-operated Italian restaurant in Nagano's Zenkoji temple gateway district, open since at least the early 2010s. The owner trained in Italy and brought back a Tuscan sensibility focused on letting quality ingredients speak for themselves.
bio.specialty: Seasonal vegetable and seafood pasta; carpaccio; Tuscan-style antipasti
bio.philosophy: Simple yet authentic Italian — coaxing maximum umami from each ingredient rather than masking flavors with heavy sauce.
bio.origin: Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan

anecdotes.text: Listed under both 'ラ・トスカ' and 'オステリア・ラ・トスカ' across directories, suggesting the name evolved informally over time while the place itself stayed small and neighborhood-focused.
With only six tables and a cash-only policy, La Tosca operates entirely on the owner's own terms — no apps, no cards, just honest Tuscan cooking a short walk from one of Japan's most-visited temples.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Pasta and pizza are staple menu items; wheat flour is central to the kitchen. Cross-contamination from pasta water and shared surfaces is a near-certainty in a small six-table kitchen. No gluten-free alternatives are listed.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy-sauce-based dishes are listed on the Italian menu, but soy sauce with wheat is not the primary concern here — pasta and pizza dough are the principal gluten sources.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Meat, seafood, cheese, and butter are used throughout the menu. Shared pans and surfaces make vegan cross-contact highly likely in a small kitchen with no stated vegan protocols.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information about allergy handling protocols was found on any source. Given the small size and cash-only, no-reservations-required format, advance phone inquiry in Japanese is strongly recommended for anyone with celiac disease or severe allergies.

caveats: Hours and Sunday dinner closure sourced from multiple directories with slightly differing details; call ahead to confirm current schedule. Seat count (24 total) from deli-koma may include bar/counter seating; Tabelog notes 6 tables. No official website found. Cash only confirmed by deli-koma. No GF or vegan information exists anywhere in the public record for this restaurant.

lead: タイレストラン トムヤムクン
name_ja: タイレストラン トムヤムクン
cuisine: タイ料理 / Thai
address_ja: 長野県長野市権堂町2210-8 レッドコアビル 4F
neighborhood: 権堂町 / Gondo, Nagano City
geocode_note: GSI geocoder matched 長野県長野市大字鶴賀権堂町２２１０番地; coordinates place the restaurant 990m from Zenko-ji, within the 1.61km target radius. Building floor (4F) not geocoded separately.
seats_text: Not listed on current Tabelog page. Previous location (same operator) had approximately 40 seats.
hours_raw: Hours not listed on current Tabelog page. Previous location operated 11:00–23:30, closed Thursdays. Current schedule unconfirmed — call ahead.
closed_days: Not confirmed for current location. Previous location closed Thursdays.
cultural_comfort_note: A Thai-run spot in a local entertainment district; menus and signage are in Japanese, minimal English expected, but the food speaks for itself.
vegan_detail: Fish sauce (nam pla) is a foundational ingredient across virtually every dish — pad gapao, curries, soups — making true vegan ordering extremely difficult. No plant-based menu section or vegan labeling found. Inquiring in Japanese is possible but outcomes are uncertain.
gf_detail: Thai cooking relies primarily on fish sauce rather than wheat-based soy sauce, which gives it a structural advantage over Japanese cuisine for gluten-avoidance. However, Japanese-market oyster sauce and some curry pastes can contain wheat, and no dedicated GF policy or allergen menu has been found for this restaurant. Cross-contamination risk in a small kitchen is unverified. Celiac diners should call ahead in Japanese and ask dish by dish.

bio.background: Originally opened in July 2016 at 上千歳町 near Shiyakusho-mae Station, then relocated to 権堂町2210-8 (Red Core Building 4F), a short walk from Gondo Station in Nagano's entertainment district. The restaurant was founded on the concept of authentic Bangkok-style Thai cooking.
bio.origin: Bangkok, Thailand (chef background)
bio.specialty: Tom Yam Kung (spicy-sour prawn soup) and crab curry; lunch sets at ¥950 covering green curry, pad gapao gai, and the namesake soup.
bio.philosophy: Authentic Thai flavors without modification for Japanese palates, using a chef trained in Bangkok.

anecdotes.text: Early reviewers noted complimentary lychee served as dessert after meals — a small touch that signaled the owner's hospitality.
The original opening attracted attention for bringing Bangkok-trained cooking to Nagano, with a sister shop selling Thai groceries and household goods next door.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free kitchen or allergen separation confirmed. Small kitchen environment with no published allergen protocol.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Thai cooking traditionally uses fish sauce (nam pla) rather than wheat-based soy sauce for seasoning, reducing the main wheat vector compared to Japanese cuisine. However, some Thai curry pastes and Japanese-market condiments used in Thai restaurants can contain wheat — not verified for this kitchen.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish sauce is used across all savory dishes. No plant-based cooking separation noted.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling policy found. Staff communication is likely Japanese-only; detailed allergy dialogue may be challenging.

positives.text: Fish-sauce-based seasoning (rather than wheat soy sauce) is structurally more GF-friendly than most Japanese restaurant kitchens, giving a reasonable baseline for wheat avoidance on dishes verified dish-by-dish.

caveats: This restaurant recently relocated from 上千歳町 to 権堂町 (Red Core Building 4F). The new Tabelog listing has no hours, seat count, payment info, or reviews — data above for hours/seats/cash-only is carried forward from the previous location listing and may have changed. Verify by phone (080-6935-9651) before visiting.

name_ja: パスタと自然派ワイン こまつや
cuisine: Italian pasta, natural wine
address_ja: 長野県長野市西之門町500-8
geocode_note: GSI geocode on 長野県長野市大字長野西之門町 returned [138.187027, 36.65831]; approximately 0.35km from Zenkoji center — well within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 善光寺下駅 / 西之門町
seats_text: 13 seats, mostly counter overlooking the open kitchen with a few table spots
hours_raw: Lunch weekdays 11:30–14:30 LO (close 15:00); lunch weekends/holidays 11:30–14:00 LO (close 15:00). Dinner Fri–Sun and holidays 18:00–21:00 LO (close 22:00); weekday dinner by party reservation (4+ guests) only. Closed every Wednesday and 2nd & 4th Tuesdays.
closed_days: Every Wednesday; 2nd and 4th Tuesdays
cultural_comfort_note: Staff can accommodate some dietary requests and at least one TripAdvisor reviewer noted an English menu is available, though the tiny counter setting is very Japanese in pace and style.
gf_detail: This is a pasta-focused Italian restaurant. The house specialty is handmade pasta using locally milled Shinshu wheat flour (地粉), which contains gluten. No gluten-free pasta or alternatives are documented anywhere. Soy sauce may appear in some preparations; no allergen menu is published. Celiac diners should avoid.
vegan_detail: The menu is heavily vegetable-forward — Shinshu organic produce features in most pasta dishes — but meat and fish pasta are also standard. TripAdvisor reviewers confirm staff can modify dishes on request (e.g. removing sausage). Hidden animal ingredients are likely in pasta sauces, bread, and wine pairings. No published vegan menu; ask when booking via LINE or phone.

bio.chef_name: Hiromasa (owner/chef, first name only confirmed in sources)
bio.background: The owner is a former indie rock drummer from Yamagata Prefecture who released CDs and toured nationally. He gained culinary experience working at the D&DEPARTMENT project cafes in Tokyo and Osaka — design-lifestyle spaces known for slow, considered food — before relocating to Nagano and opening this restaurant with his wife Aya.
bio.origin: The space was formerly the Komatsuy Arakumono Zakka-ten (小松屋荒物雑貨店), a hardware and sundries shop that had operated since the Edo period in the quiet alley one block behind Zenkoji's main approach. The owner discovered it during a visit to his wife's family home and saw potential where others saw only a commercially dead back-street.
bio.specialty: Handmade pasta using Shinshu locally milled wheat flour, dressed with regional organic vegetables and sauces such as pancetta-onion-mascarpone tomato or fresh basil tomato with Shinshu pork loin. A rotating monthly pasta special anchors the menu alongside daily options.
bio.philosophy: Prioritizes what is 'truly delicious' without pretension or shortcuts — locally sourced organic Shinshu vegetables, handmade pasta from regional flour, Shinshu miso, and an all-natural-wine list that highlights Nagano prefecture producers. Inspired by D&DEPARTMENT's ethos of 'long-life design': quality and authenticity over trend-chasing.

anecdotes.text: The owner found the former hardware shop during a marriage-introduction visit to his wife's family home. He was drawn to the quiet alley location — dismissed as commercially disadvantaged by others — and saw it as exactly the kind of under-the-radar spot worth inhabiting.
Homemade bread served alongside pasta is described by multiple visitors as 'exquisite' and a highlight of the meal.
On TripAdvisor, a diner noted that staff accommodated a vegetarian family member with 'fantastic' service, though they cautioned that not all ingredients are listed on the menu.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The kitchen makes all pasta in-house using Shinshu wheat flour. There is no documented gluten-free preparation area and cross-contamination with wheat is essentially unavoidable.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No allergen menu is published. Soy sauce (which typically contains wheat) may appear in some preparations; the restaurant has not disclosed ingredient-level details publicly.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Animal-based ingredients (meat, fish, cheese, eggs, butter) are present throughout the kitchen. No separation for vegan preparation is indicated.

staff_allergy_handling.text: TripAdvisor reviewers confirm staff can modify dishes on request — for example removing sausage from the porcini tagliatelle — when informed in advance. Reservations via LINE or phone allow advance communication of restrictions.

positives.text: The menu is genuinely vegetable-forward: Shinshu organic produce features prominently in most dishes, and the kitchen uses locally sourced organic ingredients throughout.
Reservations via official LINE (lin.ee/b64Mej3) allow guests to communicate dietary needs before arrival, and the intimate 13-seat counter setting means staff are very attentive.

caveats: Closed days have been reported inconsistently across sources: most reliable (penne.jp, reallocal.jp) say every Wednesday + 2nd & 4th Tuesdays; Retty lists 2nd & 4th Thursdays instead of Tuesdays. Confirm before visiting. Dinner service on weekdays requires a group of 4+ by advance reservation. Hours_week entry for Tuesday reflects the typical open Tuesday (1st, 3rd, 5th); on 2nd & 4th Tuesdays the restaurant is closed — treat all Tuesday visits as needing advance confirmation.

lead: 信州門前ベーカリー 蔵
name_ja: 信州門前ベーカリー 蔵
cuisine: ベーカリー・ホットサンド
address_ja: 長野県長野市長野西之門町500-12
geocode_note: GSI geocode on 長野県長野市大字長野西之門町５００番地; result lat 36.65831 lng 138.187027 — approx 343 m south of Zenko-ji reference point, well within 1.61 km
neighborhood: 善光寺門前 / 西之門町
seats_text: 20席 — eat-in seating on the second and third floors of the historic kura building; bread purchased on the ground floor
cultural_comfort_note: All signage and menus are in Japanese; no English-language service has been noted at this small artisan bakery.
gf_detail: This is a conventional wheat bakery. All breads are made with Nagano Prefecture wheat flour and Northern Alps water; no gluten-free products or dedicated gluten-free preparation area has been mentioned in any source. Standard soy sauce may appear in sandwich fillings. Not suitable for celiac diners.
vegan_detail: Breads and hot sandwiches contain butter, eggs, dairy, and meat fillings (venison curry, sausage, ham-and-cheese croque monsieur). Anpan uses traditional red bean paste but the dough itself is not vegan. No plant-based or vegan menu items have been identified.

bio.background: Operated under 株式会社東翔 (Tosho Foods), the bakery opened on December 23, 2021 inside a 120-year-old Meiji-era earthen storehouse (土蔵) adjoining the south gate of Zenko-ji's Daihongan precinct. The kura structure — original timber beams, clay walls, and lattice doors — was carefully renovated to blend heritage character with contemporary display cases and lighting.
bio.specialty: 門前角パン (Monzen Kakupan) — a square-shaped specialty loaf in various sweet fillings — and 信州門前さんど, hot sandwiches built with regional ingredients such as wild Shinshu venison curry, local pickled turnips, and Nagano apples. Premium shokupan is made with soft water drawn from the Northern Alps.
bio.philosophy: Head baker Maruyama's stated aim is to create 'approachable, unique breads only found at this bakery,' incorporating Shinshu character through local flour, alpine water, and long cold-fermentation techniques. The shop's guiding phrase: '歴史と味わいが合わさった、信州の贅沢' — 'the luxury of Shinshu, where history and flavour come together.'

anecdotes.text: All bread is baked at sister location Cafe Winds Daimon and delivered fresh, with new batches typically arriving at 10 am and 2 pm. The shop often sells out before the 4 pm closing time.
The monzen anpan uses red beans sourced from Miyashita Seian, a heritage bean supplier in business since 1904, grounding the bakery's modern breads in long Shinshu food traditions.
The bakery has appeared on SBC TV (2023) and a BS11 travel programme, drawing visitors from beyond the region to the Zenko-ji approach.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Standard wheat bakery environment — wheat flour is the primary ingredient throughout the entire production process. Cross-contamination with wheat is essentially certain. Not suitable for anyone with celiac disease or serious gluten sensitivity.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No allergen menu is published. Hot sandwich fillings (venison curry, croque monsieur, sausage baguette) likely contain wheat-based soy sauce and other gluten-bearing condiments, but specifics are not disclosed.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Butter, eggs, dairy, and meat are core ingredients used throughout the bakery's product range. There is no plant-based section or allergen separation protocol mentioned in any source.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen handling procedures or staff allergy training information has been published by the bakery. Contact the shop directly at 050-8883-6606 for specific dietary enquiries.

caveats: Closed days: the company's own page (tosho-foods.com) states 火・水曜定休 (Tue+Wed closed); Tabelog and Retty show only Wednesday as closed. Tuesday closure is likely accurate per primary source but may reflect an older schedule — call ahead to confirm. Hours listed as 10:00-16:00 but the shop regularly sells out before closing; early arrival recommended. The official Wix site (windsnagano.wixsite.com) returned a 404 at time of research; pan-kura.com is the current official URL.

cuisine: 自家焙煎コーヒー、手作りケーキ・焼き菓子
address_ja: 長野県長野市大字長野立町981
geocode_note: Coordinates from Yahoo Maps listing; ~523m from Zenkoji, well within 1.61km radius. GSI address search returned only city-level result.
neighborhood: 善光寺門前 / 立町
seats_text: 15席（2階の喫茶スペースに6テーブル）
closed_days: 日曜・月曜
cultural_comfort_note: Small owner-run café with no English signage or menu reported; the intimate, quiet atmosphere is welcoming but Japanese-language navigation is expected.
vegan_detail: Black coffee is naturally vegan. All cakes and baked goods are handmade in-house and typically contain dairy (cheesecake, pound cake, panna cotta). No plant-based alternatives or vegan labeling have been reported. Inquire directly if dairy-free options are needed.
gf_detail: Black coffee is inherently gluten-free. All cakes and pastries are baked in-house — no ingredient lists or allergen disclosures are publicly available, and flour-based baked goods are standard on the menu (pound cake, shortcake, financiers). No dedicated gluten-free options or celiac-safe protocols have been mentioned in any source. Soy sauce is not part of the menu. Customers with celiac disease should contact the shop before visiting.

bio.background: Hitoshi Hirano spent his student years traveling roughly 50 countries across Central and South America, Asia, and Europe, developing a deep love of coffee culture along the way. He established a guesthouse in Sapporo in 2008 and then opened a book café there in 2010. Beginning in 2013 he taught himself coffee roasting, and in 2016 he relocated to Nagano and opened 平野珈琲 on New Year's Eve — renovating the roughly 100-year-old machiya alongside friends. In 2019 he earned his CQI Q Arabica Grader certification (an internationally recognised coffee quality credential).
bio.specialty: 自家焙煎コーヒー (self-roasted specialty coffee) — 14–15 varieties of single-origins and blends roasted almost daily on a Fuji Royal machine, alongside seasonally changing handmade cakes and pastries designed to pair harmoniously with the coffee.
bio.origin: 長野市立町 / 善光寺門前
bio.philosophy: Hirano sources beans by building direct relationships with trustworthy producers worldwide, visiting farms when possible to understand each grower's practices. He roasts to bring out the unique character of each bean. The baking team uses simple techniques, adjusts recipes seasonally, and refines flavors continuously — the guiding idea being restraint and harmony with coffee rather than dessert showmanship.

anecdotes.text: Hirano and friends renovated the 100-year-old wooden machiya themselves, opening on New Year's Eve 2016. The second floor café retains salvaged materials — gym floor planks, church and school chairs — and vintage speakers playing jazz records. Antique clocks, retro cameras, and mechanical objects fill the shelves, and reviewers consistently note that 'time seems to stand still' in the space.
In 2019 Hirano passed the CQI Q Arabica Grader exam, adding an internationally recognised professional credential to what had been a self-taught roasting practice.

The shop has since announced a second location — 平野珈琲 山の喫茶室 (Mountain Tea Room) — opening in the Iizuna highlands roughly 20 minutes from the main shop, suggesting the brand is quietly expanding while keeping its handcrafted ethos.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information available on cross-contamination controls. The kitchen produces flour-based cakes and pastries daily; no allergen separation protocols have been reported in any source.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Soy sauce does not appear in the menu at all — this is a coffee and cake shop with no savory cooking.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All baked goods appear to contain dairy; no plant-based alternatives have been mentioned. Cross-contact between dairy-containing pastries and any theoretical vegan order cannot be ruled out.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling protocols or allergen menus have been reported by any source. The shop is a small two-person operation; direct inquiry by phone (050-3699-7897) or via Instagram (@hirano_coffee) before visiting is strongly recommended for anyone with serious dietary restrictions.

positives.text: Plain black coffee (all varieties) is naturally gluten-free and vegan — the shop stocks 14–15 bean varieties and roasts almost daily, so coffee-only visits are low-risk.

caveats: Hours are marked irregular because Tuesday opens two hours later (13:00) than Wed-Sat (10:00), and the official EC site posts specific monthly closure calendars that add extra days off beyond the standard Sun/Mon pattern — always check Instagram (@hirano_coffee) or the official site before visiting. The gf_confidence rating of 'ask' reflects a kitchen that bakes wheat-flour pastries daily with no published allergen protocols; coffee-only visits are safer. Coordinates sourced from Yahoo Maps listing; GSI geocoder returned only city-level precision.

cuisine: フレンチ (Casual French, seasonal Shinshu vegetable-forward)
address_ja: 長野県長野市大字長野横沢町749-1
geocode_note: GSI geocode on full address 長野県長野市大字長野横沢町749-1 returned [138.184265, 36.661572]; approx 0.27 km from Zenkoji target center, well within 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 善光寺下駅近 / 横沢町
seats_text: 20 seats (8 counter, 12 table)
closed_days: 水曜日、第1・第3火曜日
cultural_comfort_note: Small neighborhood French bistro run by two people; comfortable for Japanese-speaking diners, limited English likely, but a welcoming and unpretentious atmosphere.
vegan_detail: No dedicated vegan menu. The kitchen leans heavily on seasonal Shinshu vegetables, but courses use butter, cream, meat, and fish throughout. One source noted that dietary restrictions can be accommodated if communicated in advance, so vegan adjustments may be possible on request — ask when booking.
gf_detail: No dedicated gluten-free menu. Standard French kitchen: house-made bread served at every meal, cream sauces may contain flour as a thickener, and soy sauce (wheat-based) appears in vinaigrette preparations (e.g., confirmed soy-sherry vinaigrette on the menu). Cross-contamination risk is unquantified. One source confirms allergy requests can be communicated in advance for menu adjustments. Celiac diners should call ahead and ask specifically about flour/shoyu in each course component.

bio.background: Fufure (formerly fufule Kitchen) opened in 2014 near Zenkoji Temple in Nagano City and has been run by a two-person team managing both cooking and front-of-house. The name change from フフレキッチン to フフレ appears on Tabelog as a rebranding, with the restaurant continuing at the same address.
bio.specialty: Seasonal prefecture-style French prefix courses built around Shinshu (Nagano) vegetables — roughly 85% of ingredients are locally sourced; the rotating prix-fixe menu changes every two months.
bio.philosophy: Casual, unpretentious French dining in a relaxed setting — 料理も内装もオシャレだけど気取りがない (sophisticated food and decor, without pretension). The kitchen prioritizes fresh local produce and lets seasonal ingredients speak for themselves.

anecdotes.text: Opened in 2014 near Zenkoji and built a loyal following among both local residents and visitors from across Nagano Prefecture.
Menu rotates every two months to track Shinshu seasons, with over 80-85% of ingredients sourced from within Nagano Prefecture.
The two-person operation — one cooking, one serving — maintains consistent hospitality described as friendly and warm throughout the meal.

bio.origin: 長野市 / Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free kitchen or protocols mentioned across any reviewed source. Standard French kitchen with house-made bread at every service.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Soy sauce confirmed in at least one vinaigrette preparation (soy and sherry vinegar vinaigrette on seared tuna appetizer), indicating standard wheat-containing shoyu is used.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dairy (cream, cheese, butter) and animal proteins are core to most course components; no vegan-separated prep line noted.

staff_allergy_handling.text: One source confirms that if dietary restrictions or food allergies are communicated in advance, the kitchen will adjust individual dish contents accordingly.

positives.text: Vegetable-forward philosophy with over 85% locally sourced Shinshu produce means many course components are plant-based at their core.
Small two-person operation makes customization more feasible than at a large restaurant — direct communication with the kitchen is straightforward.

caveats: Hours on Tabelog (current) show dinner LO at 19:30, but older blog sources show LO 20:30 — the Tabelog figure is treated as current. Tuesday closure pattern is irregular (some Tuesdays dinner-only, 1st+3rd Tuesdays fully closed), so call ahead. Payment: Tabelog lists cards (VISA/Master/JCB/AMEX/Diners) + PayPay; an older blog from 2015 said cash only — the current Tabelog data supersedes that. No chef name found in any public source.

cuisine: ダイニングバー、ロティサリーチキン、ローカルクラフトビール
neighborhood: 権堂町 / 善光寺より徒歩約10分
address_ja: 長野県長野市大字鶴賀権堂町2300
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch resolved 長野県長野市大字鶴賀権堂町２３００番地 to [138.188019, 36.652721]; 968m from Zenkō-ji, within target box and 1.61km radius.
vegan_detail: No dedicated vegan menu. Standard dining-bar offerings include rotisserie chicken, jerk chicken, garlic shrimp, and butter chicken curry. No plant-based dishes are advertised. Dashi, butter, and meat are central to the menu. Guests with strict requirements should contact the restaurant in advance.
gf_detail: No gluten-free options are listed or advertised anywhere. The menu includes wheat-containing items throughout: soy sauce is standard in the kitchen, beer is a core offering, and pasta and quiche appear on the lunch menu. The restaurant has noted it can accommodate allergy requests when notified in advance, but there is no dedicated fryer or GF protocol confirmed. Cross-contamination risk is significant in this open kitchen dining-bar format. Celiac diners should call ahead and verify specific dishes.
closed_days: 月曜・火曜
seats_text: 30席（カウンター・テーブル・ソファ）+ テラス約20席（ペット同伴可）
cultural_comfort_note: English menu available; online reservations accepted via HotPepper; staff described as welcoming and attentive to guests' needs.

bio.background: Opened in April 2015 inside a 100-year-old former kimono wholesaler's building (呉服店) in Nagano's Gondo entertainment district. The name GOFUKU is a double pun: 呉服 (gofuku) means traditional kimono fabric, nodding to the building's past, while 五福 (gofuku) means five blessings. The complex was extensively renovated to reveal its Meiji-era wooden structure while introducing contemporary design elements. Beyond the restaurant itself, the property houses a patisserie, hair salon, nail salon, vintage clothing shop, and shared office spaces, functioning as a community hub.
bio.specialty: Rotisserie chicken marinated in house-made spices and slow-roasted whole in a dedicated oven — crispy outside, juicy inside — served alongside a rotating selection of around 25 local Nagano craft beers.
bio.philosophy: Manager Tokumaaru Emi, who has run the restaurant since opening, describes it as a place where the warmth comes not just from food and space but from the relationships between guests, staff, suppliers, and neighboring businesses. The goal is an atmosphere people return to.
bio.origin: Gondo-cho, Nagano City — the historic entertainment district one stop from central Nagano on the Nagaden line.

anecdotes.text: The building is not just a restaurant but a shared public space: alongside GOFUKU, a patisserie, hair salon, nail salon, vintage clothing shop, and shared offices all occupy the renovated 100-year-old compound, making it a small neighborhood of its own inside Gondo.
The rotisserie oven renders excess fat while keeping the chicken moist — the kitchen's signature investment and the dish that defines the menu.
During summer 2025 exterior renovation work, the terrace was temporarily closed, showing the restaurant's commitment to ongoing upkeep of the historic structure.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free preparation area or protocol is mentioned anywhere. This is a full dining-bar kitchen handling pasta, quiche, beer-battered or marinated meats, and wheat soy sauce as standard ingredients.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard soy sauce (wheat-containing) is assumed to be used throughout the kitchen; no tamari or GF soy sauce alternative is mentioned on any menu or review source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Rotisserie chicken, jerk chicken, and garlic shrimp are core menu items; butter and dairy appear in lunch sets. No separation of vegan prep is indicated.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Search results indicate the restaurant can accommodate food allergies when notified in advance, but no formal allergy menu or written protocol has been confirmed.

positives.text: English menu available, lowering the communication barrier for allergy discussions with staff.

caveats: Hours vary by day of week — Sunday is lunch-only. Terrace seating underwent renovation in 2025 and may have limited availability. Dinner carries a ¥300/person table charge. Always call ahead for allergy accommodation requests.

cuisine: 精肉・洋食 (Butcher shop with Western-style lunch; beef cutlet set meals and sandwiches)
neighborhood: 善光寺下 / 東之門町 (Zenkoji Higashi-no-mon-cho)
address_ja: 長野県長野市長野東之門町429
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch on 長野県長野市大字長野東之門町４２９番地. Confirmed within target box and 378m from Zenkoji reference point.
seats_text: 12 table seats in a small dine-in area (lunch service only)
hours_raw: 10:00–18:30 (Tabelog); lunch service 11:30–14:00 LO. Closed Sundays and on the 1st of each month.
closed_days: Sundays; also the 1st day of each month (open on the 31st)
cultural_comfort_note: A working neighborhood butcher shop with a small lunch counter near Zenkoji; primarily Japanese clientele with no English menu or signage noted, but straightforward counter ordering.
vegan_detail: This is a meat-specialist butcher and Western lunch spot — the entire menu centers on beef, pork, and chicken. There are no plant-based mains. Croquettes and sides may contain meat or animal-based fillings. Not suitable for vegans.
gf_detail: The signature beef cutlet (zabuton) is breaded with wheat panko and fried in a shared fryer. Sauces (balsamic, ginger, house dressing) have unknown ingredients — wheat-based soy sauce is common in such preparations. The shop is a traditional butcher/deli with no gluten-free menu or allergen guidance published. Not suitable for celiac diners.

bio.background: A family-run butcher shop founded in 1948 in the approach lane to Zenkoji Temple's East Gate. The business passed through three generations, with Ishizaka Yasushi — the third-generation owner — later opening a separate Western restaurant called USHIMI洋食店 in the Nitta-machi area in 2018. After his father's death in 2022 and the closure of USHIMI洋食店 on March 15, 2023, Yasushi consolidated both businesses into the original butcher shop, reopening on May 9, 2023 as a combined butcher, deli, and Western lunch spot. His stated philosophy: 'To survive the times, we must adapt and try new things.'
bio.origin: 長野市長野東之門町 (Higashi-no-mon-cho, Nagano City) — near the eastern approach to Zenkoji Temple
bio.specialty: Zabuton beef cutlet (牛カツ定食) — a rare, lightly marbled cut of beef breaded and fried to a pink medium-rare, served with ginger sauce, mustard, and Yawataya Isogoro spice blend, plus salad, soup, and a drink. Also the Ushimi Sandwich (Ushimiサンド) featuring the same beef in a sandwich format.
bio.philosophy: Artisanal neighborhood butchering focused on quality domestic and Shinshu beef; the lunch counter extends that same beef-first philosophy to plated meals, bringing accessible quality to temple pilgrims and local regulars alike.

anecdotes.text: After his father's death in 2022, Yasushi chose to merge his popular Western restaurant into the original family butcher shop rather than close either. He framed it simply: 'To survive the times, we must adapt and try new things.'
The reopening in May 2023 drew immediate excitement from regulars who had mourned the March closure of USHIMI洋食店 — many rushed back for 'that beef cutlet sandwich' they thought was gone for good.

gf_cross_contamination.text: The beef cutlet is breaded in wheat panko and fried; no dedicated fryer for gluten-free items is indicated. All fried items (croquettes, cutlets) are prepared in the same kitchen.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Sauces including ginger sauce and balsamic house sauce are house-made; no information confirms they are wheat-free. Standard Japanese soy sauce contains wheat.

vegan_cross_contact.text: This is a meat butcher shop and the deli/lunch counter handles beef, pork, and chicken throughout. No plant-based items are produced in a separate area.

caveats: Hours show minor discrepancy between sources (official site/blogs say 18:00 closing; Tabelog says 18:30) — Tabelog figure used as potentially more current. Closed days include Sundays plus the 1st of each month — verify before visiting. Lunch seating is only 12 seats with no reservations; arrive early. Owner name romanization (Ishizaka Yasushi / 石坂康) confirmed via web-komachi article.

lead: 麺道 麒麟児 大門店
name_ja: 麺道 麒麟児 大門店
address_ja: 〒380-0841 長野県長野市大門町55-1 ぱてぃお大門内
geocode_note: GSI geocoder query 長野県長野市大字長野大門町５５番地 returned [138.187851, 36.655628]. Independently corroborated by Patio Daimon complex coordinates ~36.6559, 138.1876. Approximately 643m from Zenkoji — well within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 善光寺門前 / 大門町 ぱてぃお大門内
cuisine: ラーメン・中華そば
vegan_detail: All broths are animal-based: the light soy (中華そば) uses whole chicken, chicken feet (momiji), and duck; the chicken broth (鶏そば) is a rich tori paitan reduced from chicken carcasses. No plant-based or vegan options mentioned on the menu or any source.
gf_detail: Standard ramen shop with wheat-based noodles throughout. Soy sauce (shoyu) tare almost certainly contains wheat. No gluten-free options, dedicated fryer, or allergen accommodation information found on any source. Celiac and gluten-sensitive diners should avoid.
hours_raw: 11:00〜15:00（スープ・麺が終了次第閉店）／不定休（水曜閉店が多いが月により変動）
closed_days: 不定休（水曜日が多い）。毎月の営業カレンダーは公式サイト・SNSで確認のこと。
seats_text: 11席（カウンター7席、テーブル4席）
cultural_comfort_note: Tiny counter-focused ramen shop (11 seats) near Zenkoji Temple; no English signage confirmed, though the Patio Daimon complex draws foreign tourists — basic Japanese or pointing at the menu board will suffice.

bio.background: Hoshi Hirohito opened the original 麺道 麒麟児 in Nagano in 2011 after roughly 15 years training at well-regarded ramen establishments in the Tsubame-Sanjo region of Niigata. By 2023 he had built enough of a following — with consistent queues at the main shop — to launch the Kirinji Group multi-location strategy, placing this branch inside the Patio Daimon kurazukuri warehouse complex a short walk from Zenkoji Temple.
bio.specialty: Two-pillar menu: 中華そば (light soy, featuring whole chicken and chicken feet slow-simmered with a touch of duck) and 鶏そば (rich tori paitan reduced from chicken carcasses to a silky, near-porridge consistency). Noodles are house-made to pair with each broth.
bio.philosophy: Hoshi's stated aim for the Daimon branch was to bring Kirinji's ramen to Nagano's tourist heartland: 'Zenkoji is a premier tourist destination — I want to serve our ramen to visitors from outside the prefecture and international tourists.' The group tagline is '笑顔をつなぐ心温まるおもてなし' (warm hospitality connecting smiles).
bio.origin: 長野市, 長野県

anecdotes.text: The Daimon branch opened on April 28, 2023 as the Kirinji Group's first satellite location, deliberately sited inside the renovated kurazukuri (white-walled storehouse) Patio Daimon complex to capture Zenkoji pilgrimage-route foot traffic.
The original main shop had already established a reputation for some of the longest and most consistent queues in Nagano City before the expansion began.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free preparation area or fryer documented. Wheat noodles are the core product; cross-contamination is a near-certainty.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard shoyu tare is used in the 中華そば; Japanese soy sauce typically contains wheat. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternative is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Every broth on the menu is chicken- or duck-based. No plant-based broth or topping alternatives have been documented.

caveats: Closed days are officially listed as irregular (不定休); Wednesdays appear frequently as a rest day based on multiple sources but this is not fixed — always check the official site or SNS before visiting. Hours end at 15:00 or earlier when broth sells out, which on busy days can be well before that. Cash only confirmed by Web-Komachi review.

name_ja: パティスリー HEIGORO 本店
cuisine: パティスリー・ケーキ、季節かき氷、カフェ
address_ja: 長野県長野市大門町515
geocode_note: Coordinates from geocoding.jp for 長野県長野市長野大門町515; approximately 0.52km south-southwest of Zenkoji main hall, well within the 1.61km target radius. Location is on the Zenkoji omotesando approach.
neighborhood: 善光寺表参道 / 大門町
gf_detail: All kakigori (shaved ice) flavors confirmed to contain wheat (小麦) alongside dairy and eggs per the café menu allergen listings. Baked goods, cakes, scones, and pastries are all flour-based. No gluten-free menu items or dedicated GF options are offered. Celiac diners should not eat here.
vegan_detail: The entire menu is built around dairy (fresh cream, fromage blanc, condensed milk, mascarpone, Jersey milk soft-serve), eggs, and gelatin. Kakigori syrups are paired with dairy creams; cakes and baked goods contain butter, cream, and eggs throughout. No plant-based alternatives are listed.
hours_raw: 月・火・金 11:00-17:00（LO 16:00）、土・日・祝 10:00-17:00（LO 16:00）、水・木 定休日。最新営業日は公式インスタグラム(@heigoro_fujiya)で要確認。
closed_days: 水曜・木曜定休
seats_text: 36席（お子様椅子あり）。カフェは予約不可・店頭順番待ち制（1時間制）。ケーキのご予約は本店のみ対応。
cultural_comfort_note: Located on the main Zenkoji tourist approach and accustomed to foreign visitors, so basic English communication is likely manageable, though the menu and ordering system are primarily in Japanese.

bio.background: HEIGORO is the patisserie arm of The Fujiya Gohonjin, a Western-style hospitality complex on the Zenkoji omotesando in Nagano. The Fujiya traces its roots to 1648, when it operated as a honjin — an official inn reserved for feudal lords, in this case the Maeda clan of Kaga. The modern patisserie occupies a renovated building on the historic approach street, blending the estate's long-standing service culture with classical French confectionery.
bio.origin: 長野市大門町 / Zenkoji omotesando, Nagano
bio.specialty: Seasonal kakigori (shaved ice) made with Shinshu natural ice and housemade fruit syrups; classical cakes and long-shelf-life baked goods including Cake au Cube (cubic pound cakes with local fruit) and eight-variety sablé assortments.
bio.philosophy: Classical refinement rooted in heritage — elegant packaging and flavors suited for both gifting and in-house café enjoyment, reflecting the estate's centuries-long tradition of hospitality.

anecdotes.text: The main store closed in March 2020 and remained shuttered through the pandemic, reopening on March 17, 2022 with a focused lineup of baked goods before gradually restoring fresh pastries and cakes.

The summer kakigori uses fluffy-shaved Shinshu natural ice as its base; the peach edition features housemade Nagano-peach syrup, fromage blanc cream, meringue, and an Earl Grey gelato core — served with a pot of hot tea.

gf_cross_contamination.text: All café kakigori flavors list 小麦 (wheat) as a confirmed allergen. Pastry production shares the same kitchen environment used for wheat-based cakes, tarts, scones, and baked goods — cross-contamination risk is high for any gluten-sensitive diner.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce used in this dessert/patisserie context, but wheat is present throughout via flour-based pastries, sablés, scones, tart shells, and kakigori syrups. Wheat is a confirmed allergen in all tested menu items.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dairy is foundational to the menu — butter, cream, fromage blanc, condensed milk, Jersey milk soft-serve, and mascarpone appear across all product categories. Egg is also listed as a confirmed allergen in all kakigori items. There is no plant-based section or vegan-friendly preparation track.

staff_allergy_handling.text: One source notes that advance reservations (at least 2 days prior) may allow for some allergy accommodation — contact directly at 026-266-0156 or heigoro@fujiyagohonjin.co.jp to discuss specific needs before visiting.

positives.text: Allergen information is displayed on café menu items (dairy, eggs, wheat, almonds, soy listed per item), allowing diners to identify specific allergens before ordering.

caveats: Hours are irregular and the official site directs visitors to check the Instagram account (@heigoro_fujiya) for the current schedule. The café section operates on a walk-in queue system with a 1-hour table limit and no café reservations — cake pre-orders only. Kakigori is seasonal (approximately late June through August) and in recent years has been weekend-only during certain periods.

name_ja: CHAYADO 百八 善光寺門前
cuisine: 日本茶カフェ・ジェラート・和モダン (matcha café, artisan gelato, Japanese modern)
address_ja: 長野県長野市東後町4-1
geocode_note: Coordinates from official site Google Maps embed (36.6529743, 138.1874557), confirmed by Yahoo Maps (36.652984, 138.187465). Approximately 0.94 km south of Zenkoji, well within 1.61 km radius. GSI address-search did not resolve 東後町 to a precise point; coordinates from official site used instead.
neighborhood: 善光寺門前 東後町
seats_text: 11 seats; counter-style service; no private rooms
hours_raw: 9:00–18:30 (LO 18:00); open year-round with no set closed days
closed_days: なし (no set closed days; year-round)
cultural_comfort_note: Official site is available in English and targets international visitors to Zenkoji; tourist-area location makes English communication likely.
vegan_detail: Gelato is dairy-based (Kurohime Highland milk is a selling point), matcha lattes are made with milk, and honey is used in some drinks. Some fruit-forward gelato flavors (natsuhaze berry, blueberry, strawberry) may be dairy-free or low-dairy depending on the batch, but no vegan labeling or dedicated vegan options are declared anywhere on the menu. Ask staff about specific daily flavors.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu, labeling, or allergen declarations found. Plain matcha drinks and single-ingredient gelato scoops are plausibly wheat-free, but the croquet monsieur (corn bread with filling) contains gluten, and no dedicated fryer or separated preparation is documented. Standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) does not appear in the menu, but there is no allergen sheet on record. Celiacs should ask in detail before ordering.

bio.background: CHAYADO 百八 opened on December 9, 2025 in the Higashigoshomachi district along Nagano's central shopping street, a short walk from the Gondo Arcade and about ten minutes on foot from Zenkoji Temple. It is operated by the team behind centotto gelato, an artisan gelateria established in 2022 in Shinanomachi at the foot of Mt. Kurohime, known for turning hyper-local vegetables and seasonal Nagano fruits into inventive frozen flavors.
bio.origin: Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture
bio.specialty: Matcha-forward drinks paired with centotto gelato scoops — the signature Affogato Gelato (¥750) is served in a traditional soba cup, and the layered Matcha MOMOYA Latte (¥900) showcases locally grown fruit from around Nagano Prefecture.
bio.philosophy: The concept bridges temple-town tradition and contemporary craft: the name MOMOYA is the owner's surname rendered in kanji numerals (百八 = 108 = mo-mo-ya), and the three-story wooden lattice building is designed to offer two distinct rhythms — quiet morning reverence before Zenkoji pilgrims and lively evening energy from the arcade nearby. Centotto gelato's broader philosophy centres on food-waste reduction by sourcing off-spec seasonal produce from local farmers.
roles: boutique guesthouse (one group per night, upper floors)

anecdotes.text: The building's striking wooden lattice exterior was described by local media as a 'wooden fortress' appearing on the Zenkoji approach — a deliberate design statement on a street that mixes traditional storefronts with modern retail.

Gelato is sourced from centotto gelato's Shinanomachi farm-adjacent production site, where flavors like corn, natsuhaze berry, and rhubarb are made from produce purchased directly from local growers — including imperfect 'off-spec' harvests that would otherwise go to waste.
The Affogato Gelato is served in a soba猪口 (猪口 = small cup traditionally used for soba dipping sauce or sake), a small detail that ties the modern gelato format to local craft ceramics culture.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information about cross-contamination controls is published. Wheat-containing items (croquet monsieur) are prepared in the same kitchen as gelato and drinks.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce appears in the café menu (matcha drinks, gelato, corn soup). Wheat risk from this vector is low, but no allergen sheet has been published to confirm.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dairy (Kurohime Highland milk) is a core ingredient across gelato and latte menus. No separation or labeling for dairy-free items is documented.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No information about staff allergy training or allergy-handling procedures has been published. The café does not accept reservations (walk-in only), so pre-visit phone enquiry (026-217-2608) is the only available channel to ask about allergen handling before arriving.

positives.text: The menu is matcha and gelato-focused with minimal processed sauces — simpler ingredient profiles than a full-service restaurant, which reduces (but does not eliminate) hidden allergen risk.
centotto gelato's parent brand emphasises single-origin local produce and minimal-additive gelato-making, which may reduce hidden ingredient complexity.

caveats: Hours inconsistency: sources report both 9:00–18:00 and 9:00–18:30 (LO 18:00); the naganojoho source (18:30 close / 18:00 LO) is used as the most detailed. No allergen sheet or vegan/GF menu exists as of research date. Seating count (11) from a single source (web-komachi). The venue doubles as a boutique guesthouse; café and accommodation operate somewhat independently.

cuisine: カフェ / 精進粥 / 和菓子
address_ja: 長野県長野市元善町492 善光寺大勧進 紫雲閣内
geocode_note: Coordinates from Rurubu for 善光寺大勧進 (元善町492). GSI address search returned only city-level fallback; Rurubu confirmed 36.66046, 138.18676 — approximately 112m from the Zenkoji target point, well within 1.61km.
neighborhood: 善光寺大勧進 元善町
hours_raw: 7:30〜15:30（15:00 LO）、不定休（大勧進が営業カレンダーを公表）
closed_days: 不定休（大勧進公式サイトで営業カレンダー公開）
seats_text: 紫雲閣の畳座敷をテーブル・椅子席に転用。具体的な席数は非公開。
cultural_comfort_note: お寺の中にある静かな空間で、英語メニューや英語スタッフの記載なし。善光寺参拝客が主な客層。
vegan_detail: 看板メニューの寺粥御膳（¥1,500）は精進料理ベースで植物性食材が中心。ただし、だし・醤油・みりんの仕様が非公開のため、隠れ動物性成分（かつおだし・みりん等）の有無は要確認。和菓子・おしるこ等の季節限定メニューも提供。
gf_detail: 精進料理は本来グルテンを含む食材を使わないことが多いが、醤油（小麦含有）や麩などの使用が不明。グルテンフリー対応の明示なし。セリアック病の方は来店前に必ず問い合わせを（026-234-0001）。

bio.background: 善光寺の本坊・大勧進の紫雲閣内に設けられた寺院カフェ。2024年12月より、小布施栗と信州産フルーツにこだわるスイーツブランド「井筒屋鐵治（いづつやてつじ）」が運営を担い、初のカフェ形態として展開している。
bio.specialty: 寺粥御膳（¥1,500）—日替わりの精進粥に根菜の煮物・豆腐料理などが添えられるセット。小布施栗を使った栗つづり（モンブラン）や季節の和菓子・おしるこも人気。
bio.philosophy: 善光寺参拝後に身体を温め、心を整えるための食を提供。お朝事の終了時刻に合わせて開店し、参拝のしめくくりに精進の味わいを届けることを目的としている。
bio.origin: 長野県長野市元善町（善光寺大勧進境内）
roles: カフェ運営：井筒屋鐵治（Izutsuya Tetsuji）
寺院：善光寺大勧進（天台宗本坊）

anecdotes.text: 箸袋には大勧進の僧侶が一枚一枚手書きした漢字の言葉が記されており、会計後にお客自身が気に入ったものを選んで持ち帰ることができる。箸は無垢材製でお守り的な存在感があると評判。
紫雲閣の座敷から善光寺の山門・本堂と蓮池を一望できる。2024年11月のラジオ取材では「身も心もすっきり×ほかほか」と紹介され、朝の参拝後の穴場スポットとして注目された。

gf_cross_contamination.text: グルテンフリー対応や交差汚染防止策の記載なし。精進料理ベースではあるが、厨房での小麦使用状況は不明。

soy_sauce_wheat.text: 醤油（小麦含有）の使用が料理に含まれる可能性があるが、使用する醤油の種類（小麦あり・なし）について公式の言及なし。

vegan_cross_contact.text: 隠れ動物性食材（かつおだし・みりん等）の有無について非公開。精進料理の伝統的手法に従っていればヴィーガン対応の可能性が高いが、要確認。

staff_allergy_handling.text: アレルギー対応方針の明示なし。問い合わせ先：026-234-0001（大勧進）またはcontact@tsumugi-wb.co.jp

positives.text: 精進料理をベースとしたメニューは動物性食材を最小限に抑える伝統に沿っており、植物性食材中心の食事が期待できる。
寺粥御膳は根菜の煮物や豆腐など植物性食材を中心とした構成と複数のレビューが報告している。

caveats: 営業は不定休で大勧進が月ごとに営業カレンダーを公開（daikanjin.jp）。2024年12月より運営主体が「井筒屋鐵治」に変わり、メニューや営業形態が変化している可能性がある。食物アレルギー・グルテンフリー対応については事前に026-234-0001へ要確認。現地に英語スタッフの記載なし。

name_ja: モンゼンテラス エンヤ
cuisine: 日本料理・創作和食 / ダイニングバー (Japanese fusion, sushi, grilled dishes, ramen, Western-influenced)
address_ja: 長野県長野市大門町57-2 ぱてぃお大門
geocode_note: GSI geocoder matched 長野県長野市大字長野大門町５７番地; coordinates [138.187851, 36.655628] are approximately 640m south of Zenkoji — well within the 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 善光寺門前 大門町 (Patio Daimon complex)
vegan_detail: Some plant-forward items exist — vegetable tempura, avocado sushi rolls, grilled vegetables, Nozawana tempura — but the kitchen is omnivore. Ramen broth is meat-based, and all frying is done in shared oil (not segregated). Staff are noted to be accommodating with modifications (e.g., omitting mayo or meat toppings), but there is no dedicated vegan menu or allergen-separated prep.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu or labelling. The kitchen is a full omnivore operation using standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing), wheat-based tempura batter, ramen noodles, and pizza. Shared fryer confirmed. Celiac or wheat-allergic diners should call ahead; some naturally GF items (grilled meats and vegetables, plain sashimi) may be orderable, but cross-contamination risk is real and not managed by a dedicated protocol.
closed_days: 不定休 (irregular; no fixed closed day)
seats_text: 120 seats across three areas: ground floor (48), second floor (40, with semi-private rooms), and open terrace (60, dog-friendly and winterized with bubble domes)
cultural_comfort_note: English menus are available and staff are noted by multiple reviewers to speak English well and assist foreign visitors with dietary questions.

bio.background: Opened in 2018 inside the Patio Daimon complex — a cluster of traditional townhouse buildings with gardens on the old gate approach to Zenkoji Temple — Monzen Terrace Enya was conceived to bring evening vitality back to a historic district that had gone quiet after dusk. The name plays on the Japanese word for "edge" or "veranda" (縁) and the idea of a welcoming threshold lit against the night.
bio.specialty: Sushi rolls (including a signature soba-ramen-and-roll combo at lunch) alongside grilled Shinshu beef and Fukumidori chicken using locally sourced Nagano prefecture ingredients; handmade desserts including a soba-tea choux cream and Nagano apple pie.
bio.philosophy: Three-generation family dining in a relaxed, unpretentious setting — the kitchen bridges washoku tradition with Western-leaning dishes so every member of a mixed group can find something they enjoy.

anecdotes.text: The terrace runs year-round: in summer it hosts an open parasol beer garden, and in winter heated bubble domes turn the outdoor seats into an atmospheric winter dining spot — unusual for inland Nagano.
A takeout café counter opens at 9:30 AM serving the restaurant's signature SO-BAR — a soba-flavored choux cream with soft serve — before the dining room opens.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Shared fryer confirmed by a HappyCow reviewer: "everything is fried in the same oil." Wheat-based tempura batter is standard; no dedicated GF fryer.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No GF soy sauce alternative is mentioned on any menu or review source. Standard wheat-containing Japanese shoyu is assumed throughout sauces, marinades, and dipping condiments.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Shared fryer used for both meat and vegetable tempura. Ramen broth is meat-based. Plant-based ordering is possible with staff guidance but kitchen is not vegan-segregated.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Staff are praised as friendly, English-fluent, and willing to customize (e.g., omitting mayo or pork toppings on request), but no formal allergen protocol is documented.

positives.text: English menus and English-speaking staff lower the communication barrier significantly for guests with dietary needs.

caveats: Hours listed as 11:30–22:00 daily with irregular closures (不定休); some sources list slightly different lunch/dinner break times. Seat count varies across sources (120–130); 120 used as primary figure. No chef or owner name confirmed in any source. GF and vegan safety data is limited to crowd-sourced reviews rather than direct kitchen confirmation — treat gf_confidence "ask" as minimum precaution for wheat-sensitive diners.

lead: 戸隠つきや 門前店
name_ja: 戸隠つきや 門前店
cuisine: 蕎麦 (Togakure buckwheat soba)
address_ja: 長野県長野市長野桜枝町916-1
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 長野県長野市長野桜枝町916-1 returned [138.185593, 36.658413]. Within target box and ~370m from Zenkō-ji (36.6614, 138.1872), well inside 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: Zenkojishita / 善光寺門前 (Sakuraedaland-cho, near Zenkō-ji south approach)
seats_text: Approximately 24 seats with counter and table seating; private room fits up to 6.
closed_days: 月曜・火曜定休
cultural_comfort_note: Casual, welcoming atmosphere with counter seating and a relaxed diner vibe; staff are friendly but the menu and ordering process are in Japanese, so basic Japanese phrases help.
gf_detail: Togakushi soba noodles are made from buckwheat, which is naturally gluten-free, but standard Japanese soy sauce (containing wheat) is used in the tsuyu dipping broth. Tempura on the menu uses wheat flour batter. No dedicated GF protocol, separate fryer, or staff allergy training has been documented. Celiacs should inquire directly and treat with caution.
vegan_detail: The tsuyu broth is almost certainly fish-based (katsuobushi dashi), as is standard for Japanese soba. Seasonal vegetable side dishes and a vegetable croquette appear on the menu, but hidden fish dashi in sauces and broths is very likely. No vegan-specific menu or notation has been found. Ask staff specifically about dashi; navigating this without Japanese will be difficult.

bio.chef_name: Naoki Tachikawa (store manager, 立川直樹)
bio.background: The owner originally ran a traditional izakaya in Nagano before relocating to Tokyo's Nishi-Azabu district, where he opened the flagship SOBA & DINER 戸隠つきや. The Monzen branch, which opened in August 2024, is a homecoming of sorts — a return to Nagano and a location just steps from Zenkō-ji temple's southern approach. The concept blends the izakaya spirit with the soba-specialist tradition.
bio.origin: Nagano City (Nagano origin), with the flagship in Nishi-Azabu, Tokyo
bio.specialty: Togakure kirishita soba — hand-cut buckwheat noodles from fog-grown (霧下そば) highland fields in Togakushi, served in the traditional ぼっち盛り (bocchi-mori) presentation: five neat bundles arranged on a bamboo mat
bio.philosophy: Soba as sustenance and heritage: Togakushi buckwheat has been carried by mountain ascetic practitioners (山岳修行者) as their sole permitted food during training, making it part of a living spiritual tradition. The restaurant serves it alongside foraged seasonal mountain vegetables and local Shinshu sake.

anecdotes.text: The short, finely-cut noodles have a mochi-like, chewy density that surprised one reviewer — eating them felt like consuming a mass of soba rather than individual strands, a departure from conventional soba texture that divided opinion but left a strong impression.
The menu changes with the seasons: spring brings foraged wild mountain vegetables (sansai), autumn brings fresh mushrooms harvested personally by the chef from surrounding mountains.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information found about separate prep surfaces or fryers for gluten-free orders. Tempura (wheat batter) is cooked in the kitchen alongside soba, raising cross-contamination concern.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Tsuyu dipping broth for soba almost certainly contains standard Japanese soy sauce (shoyu), which is brewed with wheat. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternative has been documented at this restaurant.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish-based dashi (katsuobushi) is the standard base for soba tsuyu in Japan and is very likely present in the broth here. No vegan dashi alternative is mentioned in any source.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy handling protocol, allergen menu, or staff training information has been documented in any available source. Contact the restaurant directly at 026-233-8028 before visiting if dietary restrictions are serious.

positives.text: Buckwheat (soba) itself is naturally gluten-free, and plain cold zaru soba without dipping broth could be lower risk — though cross-contamination from shared kitchen equipment remains unconfirmed.

caveats: Hours sourced from Tabelog (most consistent across sources). Naganocitynavi listed morning hours (7:00–10:00) on Fri–Sun at opening in Aug 2024; these do not appear in later sources (Tabelog, Web-Komachi) and may have been discontinued. Seat count varies between 24 (naganocitynavi) and 25 (Web-Komachi); 24 used as the more conservative figure. GF/vegan safety rating is low-confidence because no specific allergen documentation exists — treat as a standard Japanese soba-ya with no special dietary accommodations.

cuisine: 信州蕎麦（Shinshu soba）
address_ja: 長野県長野市長野元善町484
geocode_note: GSI address-search returned [138.187317, 36.659286]; 235m from Zenkoji, well within 1.61km radius. Coordinates sit within target box (lat 36.647–36.676, lng 138.169–138.205).
neighborhood: 善光寺仲見世通り（仁王門前）
seats_text: Casual food-hall seating; no private rooms. Order via ticket vending machine at entry.
closed_days: 毎週木曜日
cultural_comfort_note: Ticket vending machine ordering, no English menu reported, and staff communication is expected in Japanese; manageable by pointing at the machine.
vegan_detail: No vegan menu. Standard dashi is almost certainly katsuobushi (bonito flake) based — very common in traditional Shinshu soba shops. Zaru soba dipping sauce (tsuyu) will contain dashi from fish stock. No plant-based alternatives mentioned anywhere. Vegans should ask 「だしは何を使っていますか？」before ordering.
gf_detail: Reviewers describe the noodles as 二八蕎麦 (niwari soba) — an 80% buckwheat / 20% wheat flour blend, which contains gluten. No dedicated gluten-free soba or 十割 (100% buckwheat) soba is mentioned anywhere. Soba tsuyu (sauce) typically contains wheat-based soy sauce. Cross-contamination risk in a shared kitchen is high. Not suitable for celiac diners.

bio.background: Kitahei has operated since 1861 (Bunkyu 1st year), making it one of the oldest surviving soba establishments in Nagano City. It stands immediately to the left of the Nioh Gate on Zenkoji's Nakamise-dori approach, a position it has held through Japan's Meiji Restoration and beyond. The interior preserves classic shokudo (food hall) character — a period detail: the restroom is signed '洗面所' (washing area) in the old style, not the modern term.
bio.origin: 長野市元善町、善光寺門前
bio.specialty: Nameko soba (なめこそば) and zaru soba; also offers herring soba (ニシンそば) and tempura soba. Soba湯 (warm buckwheat water) is served after the meal. The shop uses seven-spice condiment from Yawataya Isogoro, a renowned local Nagano spice maker.
bio.philosophy: An unpretentious, craftsman-first approach — sturdy, rustic noodles over modern presentation. The shop keeps short hours and closes Thursdays, prioritizing quality over volume.

anecdotes.text: The shinise.tv heritage dining blog (which documents Japan's century-old establishments) notes that 喜多平 is 'one of the oldest soba restaurants in Nagano City,' founded in Bunkyu 1 (1861) — a period when Zenkoji drew millions of pilgrims and the temple approach was already a bustling dining corridor.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Noodles are described by reviewers as niwari soba (二八蕎麦) — 80% buckwheat, 20% wheat. No 100% buckwheat (十割) option confirmed. A shared kitchen making wheat-blend noodles and tempura presents high cross-contamination risk for celiac diners.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Tsuyu (soba dipping sauce) at traditional Shinshu soba shops is made with wheat-containing soy sauce (koikuchi shoyu). No tamari or wheat-free soy sauce alternatives mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dashi base for tsuyu is almost certainly katsuobushi (dried bonito). No plant-based broth option found. Tempura is offered on the menu, implying shared oil with animal-derived items possible.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy menu, allergy statements, or staff allergy training is mentioned in any source. The food ticket vending machine system means limited opportunity for customization at the counter.

positives.text: Buckwheat (蕎麦粉) itself is gluten-free; if a 100% buckwheat batch were available, the base grain would be safe — but no such option is confirmed here.

caveats: Hours confirmed as 10:00–15:00 by multiple sources (shinise.tv, Retty, TripAdvisor). Tabelog initially returned 09:00–18:00 but this conflicts with other sources; the 10:00–15:00 window is more consistent and likely current. Payment: cash-primary; PayPay mentioned in one review but no credit cards. No official website beyond Facebook page found. No owner/chef name publicly documented. GF and vegan safety cannot be confirmed — this is a traditional shop with no allergy protocol.

cuisine: 蕎麦 / Handmade soba (stone-mill self-ground buckwheat)
address_ja: 長野県長野市鶴賀田町1505-8
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch returned [138.194092, 36.653912] for 長野県長野市鶴賀田町1505. Distance to Zenkoji (36.6614, 138.1872) ≈ 1.04 km — within 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 長野市鶴賀 (Gondo Station area, near Zenkō-ji north approach)
closed_days: 毎週木曜（祝日の場合は営業）
seats_text: 30席、古民家風の一軒家。店内に300kgの石臼製粉機あり。駐車場3〜5台。
cultural_comfort_note: Small neighborhood soba house that draws local regulars; no English menu or signage reported — basic Japanese or pointing at dishes works fine.
vegan_detail: No vegan menu exists. The tsuyu (soy-based dipping sauce) is almost certainly made with katsuobushi (bonito) dashi — standard for Shinshu soba shops. Accompaniments include egg roll slices and inari sushi with rice; tempura contains shrimp. Guests needing strictly plant-based should call ahead; a plain soba with no tsuyu is theoretically possible but not documented.
gf_detail: The signature 十善そば (Juzen soba) is 100% buckwheat with no wheat flour added — buckwheat itself is GF. However: (1) the tsuyu dipping sauce uses standard soy sauce, which in Japan almost always contains wheat; (2) the restaurant also serves zaru soba at 80% buckwheat (20% wheat), meaning wheat noodles are produced in the same kitchen; (3) tempura batter (wheat) is prepared in the same space; (4) no dedicated GF fryer or allergen protocol is documented anywhere. Celiac diners face meaningful cross-contamination risk and the restaurant has made no GF declarations. Calling ahead is essential.

bio.background: Founded over 80 years ago in the back streets of Nagano city center, Otaya Sukeemon has been making handmade soba in a quiet residential neighborhood a short walk from the Gondo shopping arcade, away from the main tourist flow to Zenkō-ji. About 25 years ago the owners installed a 300 kg stone mill on the premises, becoming self-sufficient in buckwheat flour and cementing a philosophy of san-tate: freshly ground, freshly made, freshly boiled.
bio.origin: 長野市鶴賀田町
