bio.philosophy: Quality ingredients above all: natural sea salt, pesticide-free vegetables, and long-fermented dough baked in a wood-fired oven. The owners' handwritten menu and personal touches in service reflect the couple's genuine care for every plate.

anecdotes.text: The shop is perpetually fully booked — early visits or advance reservations are essential, as items regularly sell out by midday.
After closing the original pizza format in late 2021, the owners offered weekend-only takeout pizza to keep regulars happy during the transition period.
The latest iteration, TIKU-Plus, has brought back sit-down wood-fired pizza alongside the sandwich counter, offering a tomato-cheese-basil pizza for 1,550 yen in a warm machiya setting.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat flour is the core ingredient of every item on the menu — pizza dough, bread rolls, and sandwich bread are all made with domestic wheat. No gluten-free alternatives exist. Cross-contamination throughout the entire kitchen is certain.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce reported in this Italian/pizza format, but wheat is omnipresent in the bread and dough base. Wheat avoidance is not possible here.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Cheese is standard on pizzas and in many sandwiches; prosciutto, bacon, and salami are common sandwich fillings. Dairy cross-contact is unavoidable in this kitchen.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy menu, allergy card, or staff allergy-handling protocol has been reported in any source. Communication is entirely in Japanese; allergy requests should be made by phone in advance.

positives.text: Ingredients are carefully sourced: organically grown or pesticide-free vegetables, artisan cheeses from small producers in Okayama, Hokkaido, and Nagano, and dough made from domestic wheat with natural yeast and natural sea salt — a clean, additive-free kitchen.

caveats: The Tabelog listing 20015660 is marked リニューアル (renovated) pointing to the sandwich shop version (20025481), which is itself listed as permanently closed. However, as of a May 2025 blog visit, the same address (東町200) is actively operating as TIKU-Plus — a return to wood-fired pizza eat-in combined with a takeout sandwich counter. Hours are irregular; always confirm via Instagram @tiku_nagano before visiting. Cash only confirmed across all sources.

cuisine: Shojin ryori (Buddhist vegetarian); Italian-fusion shojin (THE SHOJIN)
vegan_detail: Traditional shojin ryori is fully plant-based by design — no meat, fish, or animal products, in accordance with Buddhist precepts. The Italian-fusion line (THE SHOJIN) likewise follows shojin principles. No hidden dashi from katsuo or bonito; shojin dashi uses kombu and shiitake. No specific staff confirmation on record, but the cuisine category guarantees animal-free by definition.
gf_detail: Shojin ryori avoids meat and fish but commonly uses wheat-based soy sauce (shoyu) in seasoning, and some dishes may include wheat-based fu (wheat gluten cake), which is a traditional shojin ingredient. No dedicated gluten-free menu or celiac-safe protocol has been confirmed for Gyokushoin. Italian-fusion line (THE SHOJIN) may introduce pasta or wheat components. Guests with celiac disease should call ahead (026-232-2546) to ask specifically about shoyu, fu, and cross-contamination before visiting.
address_ja: 〒380-0851 長野県長野市元善町471
geocode_note: GSI geocoder resolved 長野市元善町471 only to city level (138.194824, 36.648537). Coordinates above are Zenkoji temple grounds centroid, which is the confirmed location of this shukubo — within the target 1.61km radius of the stated anchor point.
neighborhood: Moto-Zenko-cho (元善町), within Zenkoji temple grounds
seats_text: Up to 80 guests; minimum 2 people for lodging meals; day-visitor lunch requires a minimum of 5 people and advance reservation
hours_raw: Meals tied to lodging (2 meals included with overnight stay); day-visitor shojin lunch by reservation only (minimum 5 people, reserve at least 1 week in advance). No regular walk-in restaurant hours published.
closed_days: Not published; contact temple directly
cultural_comfort_note: English-speaking staff are available according to the Nagano CVB listing, making communication feasible for foreign visitors, though the experience is rooted in Japanese Buddhist temple culture.

bio.background: Gyokushoin (玉照院) is one of the 39 permanent temple lodges (永代宿坊) on the grounds of Zenkoji, the celebrated non-sectarian Buddhist temple in Nagano that has drawn pilgrims for over 1,400 years. The lodge operates as an experience-oriented shukubo, blending traditional temple hospitality with hands-on Buddhist cultural programming for individuals, families, and corporate groups.
bio.specialty: Two styles of shojin ryori: a classical Japanese set featuring bamboo-leaf sushi, sesame tofu, yuba, and vegetarian grilled dishes; and THE SHOJIN, an Italian-inflected plant-based menu that uses seasonal produce with balsamic vinegar and organic preparations.
bio.philosophy: Gyokushoin frames the temple meal as an act of mindfulness and gratitude rather than simple dining, positioning shojin ryori alongside zazen, sutra copying, and rosary-making as a path to spiritual renewal.
bio.origin: Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan — within the precincts of Zenkoji temple

anecdotes.text: Corporate teams, hospital staff, walking clubs, and sports teams have all visited Gyokushoin for shojin ryori experiences combined with zazen and other Buddhist practices, indicating the temple's breadth as a retreat destination beyond traditional pilgrimage.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on cross-contamination protocols has been published. The kitchen serves a traditional temple setting and no celiac-safe procedures have been confirmed.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese shojin ryori typically uses wheat-containing soy sauce (shoyu) for seasoning. Additionally, fu (wheat gluten cake) is a common shojin ingredient. Neither tamari nor gluten-free shoyu alternatives have been confirmed for this kitchen.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Shojin ryori is vegan by religious design — the Buddhist precepts governing the cuisine exclude all animal products from the kitchen, making cross-contact with meat or fish highly unlikely.

staff_allergy_handling.text: English-speaking staff are noted as available, which means allergy communication is possible, but no documented allergy protocol or allergen menu exists. Direct phone inquiry (026-232-2546) before visiting is strongly recommended for anyone with gluten sensitivity or celiac disease.

positives.text: Cuisine is fully plant-based by Buddhist religious mandate — no meat, fish, eggs, or dairy, making it reliably vegan without need to interrogate individual dishes.
Day-visitor shojin lunch is available (minimum 5 people, reservation required at least 1 week ahead) at approximately ¥1,500 per person, making it accessible outside of overnight stays.

caveats: No Tabelog listing found for this venue — it operates primarily as a temple lodge rather than a public restaurant, and does not appear in standard dining directories. Day-visitor meal access requires a group of 5+ and advance reservation. Hours are not published as a fixed schedule. Geocode resolved to Nagano city centroid only; coordinates pinned to Zenkoji grounds as the confirmed location. Gluten-free safety cannot be guaranteed without direct inquiry — wheat-based fu and shoyu are traditional shojin ingredients.

name_ja: 善光寺宿坊 常住院
cuisine: Shojin ryori, seasonal kaiseki
vegan_detail: Shojin ryori is inherently plant-based (no meat, fish, or pungent alliums per Buddhist precepts). However, a non-shojin kaiseki option is also available, and even the shojin course may include dashi from plant sources (kombu/shiitake) rather than bonito — this is standard shojin practice but should be confirmed at time of reservation. Hidden animal-derived ingredients are uncommon in authentic shojin but cannot be ruled out without direct inquiry.
gf_detail: No gluten-free information is publicly documented for Jojuin. Standard shojin ryori relies heavily on soy sauce (which contains wheat) and wheat-based fu (gluten cake), making celiac-unsafe preparation the default. The zenkoji.jp listing notes that dietary accommodations for allergies and religious needs are possible upon request — making direct inquiry essential before visiting. Dedicated gluten-free preparation is not confirmed.
address_ja: 長野県長野市元善町474
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch for 長野県長野市元善町474. Coordinates fall within the Zenkoji temple grounds, approximately 1.58 km from the reference point (36.6614, 138.1872), within the 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: Moto-Zenko-cho (元善町), within Zenkoji grounds
seats_text: Approximately 90 dining guests accommodated; tatami and table seating available across multiple rooms.
hours_raw: Meal seatings by reservation only; no fixed open hours for walk-in dining. Zenkoji.jp and rurubu list hours as "予約時に要相談" (to be arranged at time of booking). HotPepper lists 09:00–17:00 as a general window.
closed_days: Irregular (不定休); dining hours determined entirely by reservation
cultural_comfort_note: All booking and communication is in Japanese; no English-language service is documented, and the dining experience follows traditional temple hospitality etiquette.
caveats: No Tabelog restaurant listing was found for 常住院 — it does not appear to have a dedicated food review page, only a hotel/lodging entry on TripAdvisor. The official website (zyouzyuuin.com) returned a connection error at time of research. Hours data reflects reservation-only dining; the standard hours window from HotPepper (09:00–17:00) is a general guideline only. Minimum group size for meal-only guests is listed variously as 4+ (HotPepper) and 10+ (zenkoji.jp); confirm by phone. Prices cited range from ¥2,000–¥6,480 per person depending on course and group size; verify current rates when reserving.

bio.background: Jojuin has served as the permanent resident lodging temple (永代宿坊) of Zenkoji for approximately 1,400 years, with its principal deity being Dainichi Nyorai (Vairocana Buddha). The temple underwent major renovations in the Heisei era, creating a clean and modern interior while retaining its sacred role within the Zenkoji complex. Jojuin is one of 39 sub-temple lodgings within the Zenkoji grounds, each with its own resident priest and chapel.
bio.specialty: Shojin kaiseki multi-course meals featuring seasonal Shinshu vegetables, handmade sesame tofu (goma dofu), and the signature 'ながの御穀膳' (Nagano grain set meal) that honors traditional grains such as millet, barley, and buckwheat.
bio.philosophy: Food is prepared without meat, fish, or pungent alliums (onion, garlic, leek, chive, shallot) in accordance with Buddhist precepts. The resident priest reportedly cultivates pesticide-free vegetables that appear on the table, and the kitchen emphasizes showcasing the natural flavor and umami of each seasonal ingredient with minimal manipulation.
bio.origin: Nagano City (Zenkoji grounds), Nagano Prefecture
roles: Shojin ryori dining for non-staying guests (by reservation)

anecdotes.text: The head priest of Jojuin personally grows pesticide-free vegetables that are incorporated into the shojin meals, reflecting the temple's commitment to grounding its cuisine in the soil and seasons of Shinshu (Nagano).

Meals are served with handcrafted original Japanese sweets (創作和菓子) and matcha tea, a traditional close to the kaiseki experience that reflects Jojuin's position as a place of hospitality as much as practice.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on cross-contamination protocols is publicly available. The kitchen serves both shojin and non-shojin (non-vegetarian) courses, so shared kitchen equipment cannot be ruled out.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard shojin ryori uses conventional Japanese soy sauce (shoyu), which contains wheat. Fu (wheat gluten cake) is a classic shojin ingredient and appears commonly in such menus. No tamari or wheat-free soy sauce alternatives are mentioned in any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The kitchen is documented to serve non-vegetarian kaiseki courses alongside shojin options; shared preparation surfaces between courses cannot be confirmed as separate from available sources.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The Zenkoji official shukubo page notes that Jojuin accommodates requests related to allergies (アレルギー) and religious dietary restrictions; specifics of how these are handled in practice are not publicly documented.

positives.text: Shojin ryori is structurally free of meat, fish, shellfish, and pungent alliums by religious tradition — a strong baseline for many vegan and plant-based diners.
The resident priest cultivates pesticide-free vegetables used in the meals, suggesting high ingredient integrity and intentional sourcing.

cuisine: パティスリー・かき氷・カフェ
address_ja: 長野県長野市大門町515
geocode_note: Coordinates sourced from geocoding.jp for 長野県長野市長野大門町５１５. Verified within target box (lat 36.647–36.676, lng 138.169–138.205). Approximately 520m south of Zenkoji Temple, well within the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 大門町、善光寺門前
seats_text: 36 seats including children's chairs; café service currently operates across the street at THE FUJIYA GOHONJIN rather than in the main shop itself
closed_days: 水曜・木曜
cultural_comfort_note: Listed on Tabelog in English with English-language website content; this is a popular tourist-area patisserie near Zenkoji visited regularly by international travelers.
vegan_detail: All signature items — butter cakes, sablés, cube pound cakes, shaved ice — contain eggs and dairy as core ingredients. The shaved ice menu explicitly lists milk and eggs as allergens. No vegan alternatives are offered or indicated on the menu or website.
gf_detail: Wheat (小麦) is listed as an allergen in all shaved ice items (blueberry: milk/eggs/wheat/almonds; strawberry: milk/eggs/wheat/soy). Baked goods — sablés, butter cake, cube cakes — are wheat-based by nature. No gluten-free options are indicated anywhere on the menu or website. Not suitable for celiac diners.

bio.background: 平五郎 本店 is the pastry shop of THE FUJIYA GOHONJIN, a hospitality institution whose roots reach back to 1648 when it was founded as a prestigious honjin inn (official lodging for feudal lords) on the road to Zenkoji Temple. For generations, each successive proprietor inherited the name 'Heigoro,' and the shop carries that name forward today. The original Fujiya hosted luminaries including statesman Ito Hirobumi and enlightenment thinker Fukuzawa Yukichi; by 1925 the building had become a three-story Western-style structure, and in 1997 it received designation as a registered tangible cultural property. A major renovation in 2006 transformed the complex into THE FUJIYA GOHONJIN — a wedding and banquet venue with an Italian restaurant — and around 2012 the confectionery arm Patisserie HEIGORO opened in a neighbouring storehouse-style building on Daimon-cho, the central street leading to Zenkoji's gates.
bio.specialty: Seasonal shaved ice (summer, ¥1,800 with tea) alongside year-round baked goods: Hotel Fuji Butter Cake made with Okinawan hon-wakatou cane sugar, cube-shaped pound cakes (Cake au Cube) in apple/matcha/vanilla, and eight varieties of hand-crafted sablés.
bio.origin: 長野市大門町、善光寺門前
bio.philosophy: fuseki-ryuko — unchanging essence expressed through contemporary craft; inheriting the spirit of a 370-year-old establishment while creating confections that resonate with modern palates.

anecdotes.text: The name 'Heigoro' was not invented for a brand — it is the historic title passed down by each successive owner of the Fujiya inn since the Edo period, making every product sold here a small inheritance of that centuries-old hospitality.

HEIGORO's shaved ice has become an annual summer pilgrimage for Nagano locals; one food blogger noted visiting 'every year as a tradition' while remarking that prices reflect both the temple-town location and inflation pressures.

The patisserie was conceived as an amenity shop that could eventually serve guests if the Fujiya ever resumed inn operations — meaning every purchase subtly keeps alive the dream of reviving Nagano's most historic lodging.

gf_cross_contamination.text: All shaved ice items list wheat as an allergen; the kitchen works with wheat-containing baked goods throughout. No mention of dedicated preparation areas.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Not a savoury establishment; soy sauce is not used. However wheat is a declared allergen across all shaved ice menu items.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Eggs and dairy are core to all items; no vegan-safe preparation lines exist.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Allergen information is published per item on the website (milk, eggs, wheat, almonds, soy noted for shaved ice); no information is available about how staff handle allergy requests in person.

caveats: Café service note: as of early 2022 the café component moved across the street to THE FUJIYA GOHONJIN annex on weekdays; the main store at 大門町515 focuses on pastry retail. Hours on Tabelog (Mon/Tue/Fri 11–17, Sat/Sun 10–17) reflect the current main store schedule. The shaved ice café runs seasonally (June 26 – August 31 in 2026). Independent in character though operated by the Fujiya Gohonjin hospitality group. Category set to MOM_AND_POP reflecting its singular, locally rooted identity rather than chain status.

cuisine: カレー、カフェ、スパイスジェラート
address_ja: 長野県長野市横町86-1
geocode_note: GSI geocoder resolved only to city level (長野県長野市). Coordinates derived from Mapion listing for 八幡屋礒五郎 本店 (36.65727686, 138.18766272), which is the same complex as the cafe at 横町86-1. Approx 460m from Zenkoji target center — well within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 善光寺大門前（横町）
seats_text: 24席（ソファ席・カウンター席・グループ席あり）、ベビーカー入店可
closed_days: 年中無休（定休日なし）
cultural_comfort_note: Popular tourist-area cafe near Zenkoji with card payments and QR pay; international visitors are common, but menus and staff communication are primarily in Japanese.
vegan_detail: No dedicated vegan menu. Staff can remove shrimp from the green curry to make it vegetarian, but the base likely contains dairy (coconut milk/butter). The spice gelato has been noted as egg-free. Most desserts contain eggs. Dashi-based broths are not confirmed; curry bases include butter chicken and beef, so the only plausible animal-free option is the gelato or selected drinks. No "vegan" labeling on menu.
gf_detail: No gluten-free menu or labeling found across any sources. Curries likely use wheat-containing soy sauce or roux components — unconfirmed. The Nagano City tourism site lists "アレルギー対応" (allergy accommodation) as a service, suggesting staff can field allergy questions, but no celiac-safe protocol is documented. Cross-contamination risk in a shared kitchen is unknown. Ask staff directly; do not assume GF safety.

bio.background: Yokomachi Cafe is operated by Yawataya Isogoro, one of Japan's three great shichimi togarashi (seven-spice blend) makers, whose history in Nagano dates to 1736. The cafe opened around 2014 inside a striking red-brick building at the foot of the Zenkoji approach, with the design inspired by the historic library building of Shinshu University — a nod to the founder's childhood memories. It underwent a partial renovation in spring 2022.
bio.specialty: Spice-forward curry in three styles — black curry with braised Shinshu beef and roasted garam masala, red butter chicken curry, and green curry with shrimp and yuzu — alongside spice gelato made from ingredients grown on the company's own farm in Mure.
bio.origin: 長野市横町（善光寺門前）
bio.philosophy: Farm to table with a spice philosophy: viewing shichimi togarashi not just as a Japanese condiment but as a Western-style spice to be creatively applied across savory and sweet dishes, using Shinshu regional produce and self-grown ingredients wherever possible.

anecdotes.text: The cafe celebrated its 10th anniversary in December 2024 with a limited curry featuring 10 spices including coriander, turmeric, cardamom, clove, and fenugreek, alongside Nozawana greens dressed with house seven-spice.
Every table is stocked with eight varieties of the house spice blends, available for free tasting — an unusual touch that doubles as a hands-on product introduction.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information found about dedicated fryers or GF cross-contamination protocols. Kitchen setup is unknown.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Soy sauce usage in curries or marinades is not documented in any source reviewed. Wheat content of curry bases is unconfirmed — standard Japanese curry roux typically contains wheat.

vegan_cross_contact.text: No dedicated vegan preparation area is noted. The kitchen handles meat-based curries (beef, butter chicken, shrimp) alongside any plant-based options, so cross-contact is likely.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The Nagano City official tourism listing notes 'アレルギー対応' (allergy accommodation) as an available service, suggesting staff will engage with allergy questions. Direct inquiry at the counter is advised.

positives.text: Staff confirmed they can remove shrimp from the green curry upon request, showing some willingness to modify dishes for dietary needs.

caveats: Hours shown as 10:00-17:00 based on official site and multiple sources; the Nagano CVB page listed 10:00-19:00 (LO 18:30) which may reflect older or seasonal hours — the official Yawataya site and Tabelog both show 17:00 closing. Coordinates are estimated from the adjacent main store location (Mapion data); GSI geocoder could not resolve 横町 to street level. The cafe is owned by a corporate entity (Yawataya Isogoro), so independent=false.

lead: マルベリーデリカテッセンアンドカフェ
name_ja: マルベリーデリカテッセンアンドカフェ
cuisine: デリカテッセン、カフェ、イタリアン
neighborhood: 県庁前駅周辺
address_ja: 長野県長野市大字鶴賀上千歳町1138-5
geocode_note: GSI geocode matched to 長野県長野市大字鶴賀上千歳町１１３８番地; 1,420m from Zenko-ji reference point, within the 1.61km target radius.
seats_text: Two-floor space; up to 100 seats; private floor rental available; high chairs for children.
closed_days: 水曜日
cultural_comfort_note: The restaurant has an English name, maintains an English-friendly web presence, and Tabelog lists it in English; staff are accustomed to non-Japanese visitors.
vegan_detail: Vegetable-forward deli sides (ratatouille, broccoli with garlic oil, purple cabbage slaw) are commonly vegan, and the shop stocks vegan products including vegan chocolate. A tofu-and-lotus-root okara hamburger retort meal is listed as a vegan option. However, the core menu is omnivore (game meats, smoked salmon pasta, quiche) and individual dishes are not systematically labeled; hidden dairy or egg in deli items is plausible. Ask staff before ordering.
gf_detail: No gluten-free labeling found on the menu or website. Bread and quiche are central offerings, and pasta is a regular feature — wheat is pervasive. Some deli sides (e.g., ratatouille, vegetable escabeche) may be naturally wheat-free, but cross-contamination risk in a busy deli kitchen is unassessed and no dedicated GF protocols are mentioned. The house-made focaccia uses soy milk but still contains wheat. Celiac diners should contact the restaurant directly before visiting; this is not a GF-safe venue by default.
caveats: As of July 2026 the restaurant is switching to cashless-only for reservations while walk-ins revert to cash; confirm current payment policy before visiting. Hours on the official site differ slightly from Tabelog — official site data used here.

bio.background: Mulberry Delicatessen and Cafe opened in July 2017 in a beautifully renovated former furniture store on Showa-dori, a short walk from Kencho-mae. The chef-owner trained in Canada and New York before returning to Nagano, where they previously cooked at a prestigious city hotel. The two-floor space — soaring ceilings, warm wood, large windows — feels nothing like a typical Nagano lunch spot.
bio.specialty: Customizable deli-plate lunch: diners choose from a color-coded showcase of prepared dishes — ratatouille, escabeche, venison curry, quiche — assembled into a single plate. The rotating weekly specials lean heavily on local Shinshu produce and foraged game meat.
bio.philosophy: The kitchen champions Shinshu terroir and sustainable eating: game meats (venison, wild boar) sourced from local hunters are offered as both a culinary and ecological statement, reducing crop damage while supplying the menu. Vegetable-forward sides and house-made breads using whole and mixed grains round out a quietly health-conscious approach.
bio.origin: 長野市大字鶴賀上千歳町 (Tsuruiga Kamisenji-cho), Nagano City; opened July 2017.

anecdotes.text: A blogger visiting in September 2017 noted the house-made focaccia used soy milk instead of dairy, and praised the mixed-grain bread options alongside the deli counter — an early signal of the kitchen's interest in ingredient substitutions.
The shop posts a 'babies crying is welcome' sign, and the ground-floor counter doubles as a takeout window — regulars treat it as a neighborhood deli as much as a sit-down restaurant.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated GF kitchen or fryer is mentioned. The deli prepares quiche, pasta, and bread alongside vegetable sides in the same space; cross-contamination risk for celiac diners is unquantified.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No information on soy sauce usage in dressings or marinades. Japanese-inflected dishes on the rotating menu (gapao, green curry, game-meat preparations) may use soy sauce; no labeling is provided.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Vegan-friendly sides are served alongside meat, fish, and dairy dishes from the same showcase counter; shared serving utensils and proximity to non-vegan items are plausible concerns.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No documented allergy protocol found. Reservations can be made via LINE or email (mulberry714@icloud.com), which are reasonable channels to communicate dietary needs in advance.

positives.text: A number of deli sides — ratatouille, broccoli with garlic oil, vegetable escabeche, purple cabbage slaw — are naturally plant-based and likely wheat-free as standalone dishes.
The restaurant stocks vegan chocolate (CoCo ChouChou brand) and a vegan tofu-lotus root okara hamburger retort, signaling openness to plant-based eating.

cuisine: カフェ、パスタ、パンケーキ
address_ja: 長野県長野市大字長野横沢町723
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch on 長野県長野市大字長野横沢町７２３ returned [138.185715, 36.66267]. Approximately 194m from Zenko-ji centroid — well within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 善光寺下駅周辺
seats_text: 15席。グループ4名まで（5名以上はランチ予約のみ）。ソファ席あり。
closed_days: 月曜・火曜
cultural_comfort_note: 小規模な古民家カフェで英語メニューや英語対応の記録はなく、日本語のみの環境と思われる。
vegan_detail: パスタ（クリームソース系）とパンケーキ（バター・メープル）が主力メニュー。動物性食材が多く含まれると考えられるが、ヴィーガン対応メニューの情報は確認できない。来店前に要確認。
gf_detail: 主力メニューのパスタ（フレッシュ麺）とパンケーキはいずれも小麦粉が主原料。グルテンフリー対応メニューや専用調理器具の情報は確認できない。醤油使用については不明。セリアック症の方は来店前に必ず店舗へ確認を。

bio.background: 善光寺の西側、裕福神社に隣接する築60〜70年の古民家を改装した隠れ家的カフェ。「toque toque」はスペイン語で「響き」や「筆づかい」を意味するとされる。
bio.specialty: 銅板で一枚ずつ丁寧に焼き上げる昔ながらのふわふわパンケーキ（バター・メープルシロップ添え）。ランチタイムにはフレッシュパスタのランチセットも提供。
bio.philosophy: 靴を脱いでスリッパに履き替えて入るスタイルが示すように、おうちのような温かみを大切にした空間づくり。季節ごとにかき氷など限定メニューを加え、地域の常連客に愛されている。
bio.origin: 長野市善光寺エリア

anecdotes.text: 古民家の趣を活かした猫モチーフのインテリアと、温かい照明が「おばあちゃんの家」のような懐かしさを演出すると複数のブロガーが紹介している。
パンケーキは夏季に提供休止となり、代わりにケニア産無農薬紅茶シロップを使ったかき氷が登場する。

caveats: パンケーキは夏季提供休止。ランチLOは13:30まで。パンケーキのオーダーは14:00〜。未就学児の入店制限あり（静かに座れる場合のみ可）。現金のみ。公式サイト（toquetoque.info）は現在404エラーで確認不可。

name_ja: しふぉん＆ケーキ 菓恋
cuisine: シフォンケーキ、カフェ
address_ja: 長野県長野市長野東町198
geocode_note: GSI address search for 長野県長野市大字長野東町198 returned [138.188995, 36.656506]; approximately 0.57 km from Zenkoji target, well within 1.61 km radius.
neighborhood: 東町 (Higashimachi), near Zenkoji Temple eastern approach
hours_raw: 水〜日 11:00〜17:00（なくなり次第終了）、月・火・金 定休（祝日・イベント時は変動あり）
closed_days: 月曜・火曜・金曜（祝日・イベント日は営業の場合あり）
seats_text: 8席（古民家の小さな空間）
vegan_detail: Standard chiffon cakes are made with eggs, dairy (butter, cream), and wheat flour. No vegan menu items or substitutions mentioned across any sources. The strawberry parfait features whipped cream. Vegetable-flavored chiffon varieties exist but are not egg-free or dairy-free.
gf_detail: Chiffon cake is a wheat-flour-based product by definition. No gluten-free options or dedicated GF preparations mentioned anywhere. Celiac and wheat-allergic diners should avoid.
cultural_comfort_note: Tiny 8-seat local cafe near Zenkoji with no English menu mentioned; some tourist foot traffic from the temple area may mean basic communication is possible, but this is a Japanese-language environment.

bio.background: A compact chiffon cake specialty shop and cafe housed in a renovated 100-year-old traditional wooden machiya near the eastern approach to Zenkoji Temple. The proprietor, whose name is not publicly documented, opened the shop out of a deep personal love for sweets — a passion reflected in the name 菓恋 (Karen), which can be read as 'love of confections.'
bio.specialty: Fluffy, moist chiffon cakes made with free-range eggs; 30+ varieties on rotation with 6–8 available daily, including vegetable and Japanese-ingredient-inspired flavors. The seasonal strawberry parfait (December–May), using Nakano City strawberries sourced directly from farmers, is a signature draw.
bio.philosophy: Handmade, additive-free sweets using carefully selected seasonal and local ingredients. The shop closes when daily cakes sell out — freshness over volume.
bio.origin: Nagano City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan

anecdotes.text: The shop's name 菓恋 is a play on the Japanese word for 'cute' (可愛い, kawaii) and means literally 'love of sweets' — reflecting the proprietor's stated motivation: wanting to see the happy faces of customers eating delicious things.
The building is over 100 years old — a renovated traditional wooden house steps from Zenkoji's eastern gate, giving the interior a distinctive retro-modern atmosphere.

caveats: Hours are irregular: Tabelog shows Mon/Tue closed (Wed–Sun open), while Nagano CVB lists Fri also closed. Hours confirmed as ~11:00–17:00 but close early when sold out. Verify current closed days before visiting. No operating confirmation post-2024 found; spot appears active based on consistent multi-source listings with no closure reports.

cuisine: Italian, European pastry (pasticceria)
address_ja: 長野県長野市箱清水2-5-8
geocode_note: GSI AddressSearch returned 138.185165, 36.662895 for 長野県長野市箱清水二丁目５番８号. Within target box and approximately 0.2km from Zenkoji center — well inside the 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 箱清水 / 善光寺裏
seats_text: Very small husband-and-wife establishment; approximately 8 seats including counter seating.
hours_raw: Lunch 11:30–14:30, Dinner 17:30–21:00; closed Wed, Thu, 1st and 3rd Tue (plus irregular holidays). Fully reservation-only for both lunch and dinner since February 2020.
closed_days: 水曜・木曜・第1・第3火曜、不定休
cultural_comfort_note: Entirely reservation-based, reservation system is in Japanese (Toreta), and the restaurant is a quiet neighborhood spot with no indication of English-language service — some Japanese ability or a Japanese-speaking companion will be very helpful.
vegan_detail: The menu is a multi-course chef's-choice set (lunch and dinner) featuring fish, meat (including Agu pork), housemade bread, and pasta. No vegan menu or substitution option is publicly advertised. Hidden animal products (dashi, butter, eggs) are present throughout. Inquire at reservation time whether any plant-based accommodation is possible given the chef-driven format.
gf_detail: A wheat-containing pasta course is a standard component of the set menu, and Italian sauces and bread are served throughout. No gluten-free menu or allergen accommodation is publicly advertised. Cross-contamination risk in a small open kitchen is a realistic concern. Inquire at the time of reservation — the very small team and advance-booking format may allow customisation, but celiac diners should confirm explicitly.

bio.chef_name: Nagaoka Yuji (長岡勇士) — chef/owner; Nagaoka Kana (長岡佳奈) — pastry chef/owner
bio.background: Opened January 2019 in a quietly renovated space behind Zenkoji Temple. Chef Yuji Nagaoka trained in Italy and honed his craft at the long-established Nagano hotel restaurant THE FUJIYA GOHONJIN before striking out independently. His wife Kana, who handles all the pastry work, previously worked at Nagano patisserie HEIGORO.
bio.specialty: Seasonal Italian chef's-choice course menus built around locally sourced Shinshu vegetables, hand-caught fish (the chef fishes himself, serving yellowtail in winter and red seabream and squid in summer), and regional Italian preparations such as Catania-style caponata with swordtip squid. The pasticceria counter offers European baked goods — galette bretonne, domestic-lemon cake, cream puffs with Tahitian vanilla custard — for eat-in or takeaway.
bio.philosophy: Tiny-scale, ingredient-led cooking: a husband-and-wife team of eight seats in a herringbone-floored converted farmhouse, entirely reservation-only, where the menu follows the season and the chef's own catch.
bio.origin: Nagano City, Hakoshimizu; opened 25 January 2019

anecdotes.text: The fish on any given course may literally be what the chef caught himself — in summer, red seabream and squid; in winter, yellowtail from local waters.
Since February 2020, even cut and whole cakes from the pasticceria counter require advance reservation — a sign of just how small-scale and intentional the operation is.

caveats: Hours (particularly Tuesday closures — the pattern shifted from 2nd/4th to 1st/3rd Tuesdays in April 2022) and irregular holiday closures should be confirmed directly or via the Toreta reservation system before visiting. The course menu and pricing change seasonally. No gluten-free or vegan accommodation has been publicly confirmed; contact the restaurant at reservation time for any dietary needs.

lead: お昼ごはんとおやつ カフェ日々
name_ja: お昼ごはんとおやつ カフェ日々
name_en: Cafe Hibi (Ohiru-gohan to Oyatsu Cafe Hibi)
cuisine: カフェ
address_ja: 長野県長野市大字南長野諏訪町509
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch for 長野県長野市諏訪町509. Point falls within target bounding box and 1,584m from Zenkoji (within 1.61km limit).
neighborhood: 諏訪町 / 善光寺門前
seats_text: Approximately 10 seats in an intimate counter-and-table layout; solo diners warmly welcomed.
closed_days: 日・月・火・金・祝日（不定休あり — 営業前にInstagramで要確認）
cultural_comfort_note: A tiny, unhurried neighbourhood cafe near Zenkoji with no English signage; Japanese helps for ordering, but a relaxed atmosphere makes it approachable even for non-speakers who can point at items.
vegan_detail: The menu centres on handmade natural-yeast bread, homemade custard pudding (contains eggs and dairy), curry sets, and seasonal desserts. The cafe emphasises "body-friendly" ingredients and uses organic teas and domestic wheat, but no explicitly vegan dishes or labelling are documented in any source. Dairy and eggs appear throughout. Ask before ordering.
gf_detail: The kitchen's signature is natural-yeast bread made from domestic wheat — gluten is central to the menu. No gluten-free alternatives or dedicated prep areas are mentioned in any source. Standard Japanese soy sauce (wheat-containing) likely used in savoury items. Not suitable for celiac diners without direct confirmation; call ahead on 026-232-0239.

bio.background: Cafe Hibi opened around 2011 in a quiet back-alley in Nagano's Suwa-cho neighbourhood, midway between Zenkoji Temple and Nagano Station. The owner bakes bread from scratch each morning using domestic wheat flour and Hoshino natural yeast — the same traditional starter used in Japanese artisan baking. The blue door is the only landmark; the interior holds just ten seats and is styled in a shabby-chic palette of white, pale blue, and warm wood.
bio.origin: 長野市諏訪町, 長野県
bio.specialty: Handmade custard pudding and natural-yeast bread plates; daily menu rotates with seasonal fruit and vegetables.
bio.philosophy: Wholesome, handcrafted food made with 'body-friendly' ingredients — seasonal produce, natural fermentation, organic teas — served at an unhurried pace in a space that feels like a friend's living room.

anecdotes.text: The cafe is so small and popular that regular visitors recommend reserving via Instagram DM before making the trip, as unannounced closures are common.
Lifestyle magazine 暮らしの手帖 is kept on the counter — a quiet signal of the aesthetic the place cultivates.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information on cross-contamination controls is available from any source. Bread baking (wheat flour) is the kitchen's core daily activity, making airborne flour a realistic concern for highly sensitive individuals.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (containing wheat) is likely used in savoury dishes such as curry; no tamari or gluten-free soy sauce alternative is documented.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Dairy and eggs are used throughout the menu (custard pudding, bread, cakes); shared prep surfaces are probable in this very small kitchen.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling procedures are mentioned in any reviewed source. The cafe can be contacted by phone on 026-232-0239 to ask directly.

positives.text: Uses domestic wheat and Hoshino natural yeast; offers organic leaf teas and caffeine-free rooibos — some ingredient mindfulness is present even if allergen labelling is absent.

caveats: Hours are Wed/Thu/Sat only (11:30–17:00); the cafe has a documented pattern of unannounced additional closures — always check Instagram (@cafehibi.nagano) before visiting. Only 1 parking space; the alley entrance (marked by a blue door) is easy to miss. No gluten-free or vegan labelling confirmed; wheat bread is central to the kitchen's identity.

cuisine: カフェ・喫茶
address_ja: 長野県長野市長野横沢町726
geocode_note: GSI address search matched 長野県長野市大字長野横沢町726番地; approximately 229m southwest of Zenkoji, well within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 横沢町 / 善光寺近く
seats_text: 12席（2階が主な喫茶スペース）
hours_raw: 水〜金 8:00〜15:00、土 8:00〜16:00、日月火定休（時間は変動あり、要確認）
closed_days: 日曜・月曜・火曜
cultural_comfort_note: A small, quiet neighborhood kissaten with no English menu; basic Japanese for ordering is helpful, but the relaxed, welcoming atmosphere makes non-Japanese speakers feel at ease.
vegan_detail: No explicitly vegan dishes confirmed. The menu centers on morning sets (toast, Chinese chimaki sticky rice with pork, eggs), cakes, coffee, and a genmai (brown rice) latte. Hidden animal ingredients are likely in most dishes; ask on arrival.
gf_detail: No gluten-free options advertised. Toast sets and chimaki involve wheat; standard Japanese soy sauce likely used. No dedicated gluten-free protocol or kitchen separation confirmed. Celiac diners should ask in advance and treat this as a conventional kissaten.

bio.background: 喫茶Sirafu opened in April 2022 in a renovated two-story traditional townhouse in Nagano's Yokosawa-cho, directly across from Yufuku Shrine and a short walk from Zenkoji Temple. The owner — a music enthusiast — curates the BGM with care, pairing a Toshiba CD player fed through Bose Bluetooth speakers with records such as Judee Sill's Heart Food. The first floor doubles as a browsing nook stocked with manga and curated miscellany; the second floor is the main cafe space, with large windows flooding the room with light and framing seasonal views of the shrine torii.
bio.origin: 長野市横沢町
bio.specialty: 中華ちまきセット（もち米と豚の角煮）、レモンケーキ、のぶしな玄米ラテ
bio.philosophy: The name 'Sirafu' (しらふ) — meaning 'sober' or 'as you are' — signals the owner's invitation: come as yourself, no pretense needed. The cafe describes itself as a morning-focused spot uncommon in Nagano, emphasizing a slow, meditative atmosphere over speed.

anecdotes.text: A music writer visiting for a café-music essay noted that the owner selects BGM 'with an emphasis on comfort,' and that the interplay of Judee Sill playing softly while leaves rustled outside created a meditative, present-moment quality rare in city cafes.

The chimaki morning set — sticky rice with braised pork, boiled egg, and pickles — was flagged by multiple bloggers as genuinely unusual for a Nagano cafe morning menu, setting Sirafu apart from standard toast-only kissaten.

caveats: Hours vary across sources and may change seasonally; the most consistent pattern is Wed–Fri 8:00–15:00, Sat 8:00–16:00, closed Sun/Mon/Tue, but verify via Instagram @kissa_sirafu before visiting. No Tabelog listing found. Seating count (12) sourced from deli-koma.com aggregator. No allergy-specific handling confirmed anywhere; treat as a standard kissaten with no GF or vegan protocols.

lead: 大福とウイスキーの店 いづみや民藏
name_ja: 大福とウイスキーの店 いづみや民藏
name_en: Izumiya Tamizo — Daifuku and Whiskey Bar
cuisine: バー・ウイスキー・大福
address_ja: 長野県長野市元善町484
neighborhood: 善光寺仲見世通り / 善光寺下
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 長野県長野市元善町484: [138.194824, 36.648537]. Approximately 1.56 km from Zenkoji center (36.6614, 138.1872) — within the 1.61 km radius. Coordinates fall within the target bounding box.
seats_text: 7 counter seats plus 6 seats around a traditional irori (sunken hearth)
hours_raw: 19:30〜22:30、木曜・日曜定休、不定休あり
closed_days: 木曜・日曜、不定休あり
cultural_comfort_note: An intimate counter bar run by a fifth-generation local family; the master is described as personable and approachable, but there is no sign of English menus or English-speaking staff — a few words of Japanese or pointing at bottles will go a long way.
vegan_detail: No vegan menu or labeling. The bar's food accompaniments have evolved from daifuku mochi to items like smoked nuts, pizza, sausage, and oden — most of which contain animal products. Daifuku, if still served, typically contains dairy-free red bean paste but the mochi skin powder coating may vary. Ask the master what is available on the night; smoked nuts may be the safest plant-based option.
gf_detail: No gluten-free labeling or claims. The bar has evolved its food offering and now serves items including pizza, sausage, and oden — all of which typically contain gluten or wheat-based soy sauce. Daifuku mochi (if available) is made from glutinous rice and is traditionally wheat-free, but cross-contamination risk in a small bar kitchen is unknown. Pure whiskey on the rocks is the safest choice for celiac diners; food accompaniments should be treated with caution and queried individually.

bio.background: The building has housed the Shimizu family's hospitality business since the Taisho era, when founding patriarch Shimizu Minzo (清水民蔵) opened it as a ryokan. In 1950 the inn was renamed いづみや旅館 and continued to operate with a folk-arts café space on the ground floor. After the lodging business wound down, fifth-generation owner Shimizu Akira — who brings 30 years of professional Japanese-cuisine training — renovated the tea room and kitchen into a hidden whiskey bar, reopening in April 2022 under the original founder's name.
bio.origin: 長野市元善町 / 善光寺仲見世通り
bio.specialty: A poured whiskey with daifuku mochi as the otoshi (house appetizer); the bar stocks roughly 60 labels spanning bourbon, single malt, blended, and Japanese whisky, all priced the same whether taken neat, on the rocks, with water, or with soda.
bio.philosophy: Shimizu describes the concept as an entirely new way to appreciate whiskey — pairing Japan's distilled spirit tradition with the textures and flavours of Japanese confectionery, in a space that carries the weight of a century of Nagano hospitality.

anecdotes.text: The original founder's name, 民蔵 (Minzo), gives the bar its name — a deliberate act of return to origins (原点回帰) after three generations of operating as a conventional inn.
In winter the traditional irori hearth is lit, letting guests warm themselves by the fire while nursing a dram — a detail Shimizu preserved from the ryokan's folk-arts café era.
The first thing placed in front of every guest is a daifuku — a pillow of mochi filled with red bean paste — alongside the first pour of whiskey; guests may take the daifuku home if they prefer.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information available on kitchen cross-contamination practices; the bar operates out of a small renovated kitchen space with no GF protocols documented.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Food items such as oden typically rely on soy sauce (which contains wheat) in the broth; no tamari or GF soy sauce alternative is mentioned.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Sausage and other meat-based snacks are served in the same small bar space; vegan cross-contact risk from shared preparation surfaces is unknown.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling procedures or staff training information is documented for this venue; the master operates largely alone at a 13-seat bar, so direct verbal communication with him is the only practical route.

positives.text: Neat whiskey and spirits are naturally gluten-free; a primarily drinks-focused visit carries minimal dietary risk for GF drinkers who skip the food accompaniments.

caveats: Hours vary across sources (18:30, 19:00, and 19:30 start times cited); the Tabelog listing is treated as authoritative (19:30). The food offering has evolved since opening — daifuku may or may not still be the otoshi. Call ahead (090-9359-8872) to confirm current hours and menu before visiting. The official site (izumiyatamizo.com) returned HTTP 403 and could not be read directly.

lead: 手揉み中華そば まるもも 長野店
name_ja: 手揉み中華そば まるもも 長野店
cuisine: ラーメン
address_ja: 長野県長野市鶴賀権堂町2284
geocode_note: GSI address-search matched 長野県長野市大字鶴賀権堂町２２８４番地; ~985m south of Zenko-ji, well within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 権堂町 / 権堂駅より277m
vegan_detail: All broths are animal-based — pork and chicken simmered 10+ hours. Toppings include char siu pork, flavored egg, and fried chicken. No plant-based options noted anywhere on the menu.
gf_detail: The signature hand-kneaded noodles are high-hydration wheat noodles. The tare (seasoning base) is almost certainly soy-sauce-based and contains wheat. Fried chicken (karaage) and gyoza on the menu add further wheat exposure. No allergen information posted, no GF claims anywhere. Not safe for celiac.
seats_text: 4 テーブル席 + カウンター6席、計約24席。個室なし。
hours_raw: 月〜木 17:30〜翌3:00、金・土 17:30〜翌5:00、日曜定休、毎月第2月曜休。22時以降は5%深夜料金加算。
closed_days: 日曜日、毎月第2月曜日
cultural_comfort_note: Deep in the Gondo entertainment district, late-night ramen bar atmosphere; ordering is verbal at the counter — no English menu or multilingual signage noted, but the format (point-and-order ramen shop) is navigable for Japanese-comfortable visitors.

bio.chef_name: 河本天志 (Kawamoto Tenshi) × 渡邉保之 (Watanabe Yasuyuki)
bio.background: まるももは長野を拠点とするラーメングループ「吟屋」のオーナー河本天志と、東京の名店「中華そば きび」の店主渡邉保之による共同ブランド。両者の長い信頼関係から生まれたこのブランドは、2025年9月に金沢で第1号店をオープンし、同年12月12日に長野市の権堂アーケードに第2号店として長野店を開業した。長野店は以前「かぶら村」が営業していた場所に出店。
bio.specialty: 手揉み多加水麺を使ったあっさり中華そば（¥900）。豚・鶏・野菜を10時間以上かけてじっくり炊き出した透き通った黄金色のスープに、独特の波打ちと折り目が特徴の手揉み高加水麺を合わせた一杯。
bio.origin: 金沢発祥（2025年9月）、長野店2025年12月開業
bio.philosophy: 深夜の権堂に「一軒目の〆」や「夜中のラーメン」という文化を持ち込む。洗練された軽やかな塩分と複層的なうまみが共存するスープを目指し、手揉み麺のもちもちとした食感とのコントラストを計算した仕上がりにこだわる。

anecdotes.text: 金沢の名店「麺屋大河」グループと東京の「中華そば きび」という異なる地域の二人の名人が、長年の信頼をもとに新ブランドを立ち上げた。金沢1号店のオープンからわずか3か月で長野出店を実現するスピード展開が話題を呼んだ。
権堂アーケード内でも「ラーメンストリート」と称されるエリアに出店。かつて別の飲食店が入っていた跡地に新サインを掲げると、アーケードを行き交う人々の注目を集め、オープン前から話題になった。

gf_cross_contamination.text: Hand-kneaded wheat noodles are the restaurant's defining product — wheat flour is present throughout the kitchen. No separation of prep areas noted.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy sauce type disclosed, but standard Chinese-style ramen tare almost certainly uses wheat-containing soy sauce. No tamari or gluten-free soy sauce mentioned anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All broths are pork- and chicken-based; there is no plant-based broth option. Lard (背脂) variants mentioned. Vegan diners have no safe option here.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The official site notes customers with dietary restrictions should contact the restaurant directly (要問合せ), but no structured allergen protocol or allergy-trained staff information is documented.

caveats: Hours sourced from multiple outlets with slight discrepancies (Tabelog shows 17:00 start; official site and food blogs show 17:30). Official site figure (17:30) used as authoritative. The 2nd-Monday closure confirmed by official site. Hours subject to change — always verify before visiting.

lead: サンデー ライフ コーヒー
name_ja: サンデー ライフ コーヒー
cuisine: カフェ・喫茶
neighborhood: 南町 / 権堂
address_ja: 長野県長野市南町477-11 FLAT BAR
geocode_note: Coordinates from Yahoo Maps place page (36.654460, 138.184089). GSI address search returned only cho-level for 南千歳 (36.6452), which does not correspond to this address. Yahoo Maps pin is within target box and approximately 820m from Zenko-ji, well within 1.61km radius.
seats_text: カウンター席のみ7席。常連客が「5〜6人入ればパンパン」と表現する極小空間。
hours_raw: 複数情報源で大きく異なる。Tabelog：月・土・日 8:30〜17:00、火〜金休。reo-gourmet：木 12:00〜21:00、土日 10:00〜17:00、月〜水・金休。nagano.boooon：月・木に夜カフェあり。来店前に必ず事前確認を（090-7170-2735）。
closed_days: 火・水・金（情報源により異なる可能性あり）
cultural_comfort_note: カウンター一体型の極小喫茶店でオーナーとの会話が自然に生まれる空間。英語メニュー・英語対応の情報はなく、日本語での基本的なやり取りが必要。
vegan_detail: トースト・ホットサンド・焼きカレーパン・アップルパイ・プリンなど喫茶店スタイルのフードが中心。コーヒーゼリーには大町産の松田牛乳を使用することが確認されており、完全ヴィーガン対応の明示はない。コーヒー・紅茶・ルイボスティー等のドリンクは動物性成分を含まない可能性が高いが、来店前に要確認。
gf_detail: トースト・ホットサンド・焼きカレーパンなど小麦粉を主体とするフードが中心。グルテンフリー対応・専用調理エリア・専用フライヤーに関する情報は一切確認できない。ドリンクのみであれば小麦リスクは低いが、セリアック病の方は来店前に電話で確認（090-7170-2735）を強く推奨。

bio.background: 松本育ちで幼少期に長野市に転居したオーナーの和田幸夫氏は、大学卒業後に内装デザインへの関心を追って上京。帰郷後は建設業に6〜7年従事し、その後ホテルレストランで飲食の世界へ。カウンターバーの昼間の空き時間を間借りする形で週3日のランチ営業を4年間続け、資金と常連を積み上げてから隣接物件に独立。段階的に理想を実現するという経営哲学を持つ。
bio.origin: 長野市南町
bio.specialty: サンデーレトロプリン（固めのカスタードプリン）と日替わりストレートコーヒー。コーヒーゼリーは自家製コーヒーを固め、大北地方の百年牧場・松田牛乳を添えたもの。
bio.philosophy: 「お客様にはとにかくゆっくりしていって欲しい」。コンセプトは『Mr Sunday Trip』—世界各地からヴィンテージ雑貨を集める架空の旅人をイメージし、インテリアは自身のコレクション家具と一点物の食器で構成。

anecdotes.text: 元々はカウンターバーが夜だけ営業していたため、昼間の空き時間を交渉して間借り営業をスタート。4年かけて常連客と資金を積み上げ、隣の物件が空いたタイミングで現在の独立店舗を開いた。
コーヒーゼリーにシナモンや七味（八幡屋礒五郎）をかけて食べる提案に、訪問客が「意外な相性の良さ」に驚いたと報告されている。

caveats: 営業時間は情報源によって大きく異なるため（Tabelog・reo-gourmet・nagano.boooonでそれぞれ異なる曜日・時間帯を記載）、来店前に必ず電話（090-7170-2735）またはInstagram（@sunday_life_coffee）で確認すること。現金のみ・駐車場なし。グルテンフリー・ヴィーガン対応に関する公式情報は存在しないため、食物アレルギー・食事制限がある場合は直接問い合わせが必須。

lead: 日本料理そば懐石 紡ぎ
name_ja: 日本料理 そば懐石 紡ぎ
cuisine: 懐石・そば懐石
address_ja: 長野県長野市大門町54-1 ぱてぃお大門 蔵楽庭
geocode_note: Coordinates from Mapfan chō-level centroid for 長野市大門町 (lat 36.6562326481, lng 138.1872939641). The restaurant is at 大門町54-1 on the main Zenkoji approach road, approximately 580m south of Zenkoji — well within the 1.61km target radius.
neighborhood: 大門町（Daimon-cho），善光寺門前
seats_text: 10 seats on the second floor of a Taisho-era kura (storehouse); shoes removed at entry
closed_days: 日曜・月曜（月曜祝日の場合は火曜休）、年末年始
cultural_comfort_note: Intimate 10-seat kaiseki counter with no English menu; foreign diners have managed via translation apps, but the experience is firmly rooted in Japanese kaiseki etiquette — advance reservation and Japanese communication strongly preferred.
vegan_detail: Kaiseki courses feature venison (Miyako Plateau), Shinshu Premium Wagyu, and karasumi (cured roe). Standard Japanese dashi (likely katsuobushi/bonito-based) is used throughout kaiseki cuisine, and no plant-based dashi alternative is mentioned. One source notes vegetarian requests are accommodated with advance notice, but this is not confirmed on the official site. Do not assume vegan-safe without direct inquiry well before your visit.
gf_detail: The signature soba is 十割 (100% buckwheat, no wheat binder) — buckwheat itself is gluten-free. However, kaiseki cooking relies heavily on regular soy sauce (which contains wheat), wheat-thickened sauces, and other wheat-bearing condiments as a matter of course. No gluten-free menu or dedicated GF protocols are mentioned on any official or third-party source. Cross-contamination risk is high in a shared kitchen producing multiple courses. Celiac diners should contact the restaurant directly before booking to discuss feasibility.

bio.background: Born 1980 in Nagano City, Machida Tetsuya grew up in the region he would one day cook for. After eating his way through restaurants across Nagano and beyond, he apprenticed for five years at a respected Japanese restaurant before entering Tsurukou (つる幸) in Kanazawa — a Michelin two-star kaiseki ryotei — where he refined his classical technique. A family meal at a Michelin one-star soba-kaiseki restaurant in Kanazawa sparked his obsession with buckwheat, leading him to spend six months living in at Mokkei (木鶏) in Yamaga-mura, a village celebrated for its soba, to master the craft. His wife Yukiko (由起子), raised immersed in Shinshu's nature and community, runs the front of house with warm, attentive hospitality.
bio.origin: 長野市（Nagano City）
bio.specialty: Omakase kaiseki courses built around self-milled, 100% buckwheat soba (十割そば) made daily with Togakushi spring water, woven through seasonal Shinshu ingredients
bio.philosophy: The name Tsumugi (紡ぎ, 'to spin/weave') expresses three interlocking intentions: 縁を紡ぐ (weaving bonds) — creating joy at celebrations; 心を紡ぐ (comforting hearts) — providing solace in difficult times; 命を紡ぐ (nourishing life) — sourcing safe, honest ingredients from dedicated producers. The couple describe wanting guests to experience all six senses: sight, sound, scent, touch, taste, and the invisible warmth of the moment.

anecdotes.text: Machida's path to soba began at a family dinner in Kanazawa at a Michelin-starred soba-kaiseki restaurant. The experience stopped him: the way buckwheat and seasonal Japanese cuisine could speak to each other was unlike anything in his training. He then moved to Yamaga-mura, sleeping and working at the soba-ya Mokkei for six months until the craft was his own.

The restaurant's hachisun (eight-piece seasonal platter) featuring low-temperature-cooked Miyako Plateau venison with Nagano honey and balsamic has reportedly drawn audible exclamations from guests — the kitchen's quiet proof that Shinshu game can hold its own in a formal kaiseki setting.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No dedicated gluten-free protocols or cross-contamination controls are mentioned on the official site or any third-party source. A shared kaiseki kitchen routinely handles wheat via soy sauce, sauces, and marinades across multiple courses.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No wheat-free soy sauce alternative (tamari or otherwise) is mentioned anywhere. Standard Japanese soy sauce — which contains wheat — is the default in kaiseki cuisine, and Tsumugi makes no exception based on available information.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Courses include venison, wagyu beef, and karasumi (cured fish roe). Katsuobushi-based dashi is standard in kaiseki; no plant-based dashi is mentioned. Vegan cross-contact throughout multiple shared courses is likely without advance arrangement.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Vegetarian guests are advised to contact the restaurant in advance. For foreign-language dietary communication, guests have used translation apps. No formal allergy-handling protocol is documented.

positives.text: The signature soba is 十割 (100% buckwheat, no wheat binder), ground fresh daily from Shinshu-grown buckwheat using Togakushi spring water — the soba itself is naturally gluten-free if prepared without cross-contamination.
The kitchen sources directly from local organic vegetable farmers in Nagano Prefecture, prioritizing high-quality, traceable produce.

caveats: Coordinates are chō-level (大門町 centroid from Mapfan); the actual building is on the well-known Zenkoji omote-sando approach and confirmed within the target radius. No chef biography page was accessible at the Nagarabo URL (404); biographical details were sourced from the search snippet of that page and corroborated by the official about page. Dietary safety data is sparse — no formal allergen disclosure exists; celiac and vegan travelers must contact the restaurant directly before booking.

cuisine: 戸隠そば・手打ちそば・うどん
address_ja: 長野県長野市鶴賀権堂町2255
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 長野県長野市鶴賀権堂町2255 returned lat 36.653297 lng 138.18959 — approximately 924m from Zenkō-ji target center, well within 1.61km radius
neighborhood: 権堂 (Gondo arcade)
seats_text: 11 counter seats only; no tables, no private rooms, no group seating
closed_days: 日曜日・祝日 (Sundays and public holidays)
cultural_comfort_note: Counter-only, elderly solo owner, no English signage or menu noted — comfortable for Japanese speakers; non-Japanese diners can point at the menu but communication will be in Japanese.
vegan_detail: Dipping broth is confirmed katsuobushi (bonito) based. No vegan dashi alternative noted anywhere. Udon also served, sharing the kitchen. Not suitable for vegans.
gf_detail: Soba noodles are buckwheat but the dipping sauce (tsuyu) uses wheat-based soy sauce, confirmed as sharp and soy-forward. Udon (100% wheat) is made and served in the same small counter kitchen, making cross-contamination near-certain. No dedicated GF preparation or awareness noted. Not suitable for celiac.

bio.background: A tiny counter soba shop that has operated for over 47 years in the heart of Nagano's Gondo arcade, tucked into the entrance of the historic Nagano Shochiku Aioiza cinema — a Showa-era neighborhood landmark. The proprietor, an elderly man who runs the shop alone, relocated from Togakushi — Shinshu's most revered soba village — to bring hand-cut noodles to the city.
bio.specialty: Tororo soba: Togakushi-style soba arranged in botchi-mori (five rounded portions on a bamboo mat), served with grated mountain yam topped with an egg yolk, and a sharp katsuobushi-based dipping broth the diner adjusts with soba yu
bio.origin: Togakushi, Nagano
bio.philosophy: The owner describes his approach simply: 'No particular philosophy, but I work diligently every day to make delicious soba.' The narrow counter creates a quiet intimacy — regulars, salarymen, and film-goers sharing the same eleven stools.

anecdotes.text: Celebrity autographs line the walls, evidence of a long-running local reputation that extends well beyond the arcade neighborhood.
The menu includes 大まくら (Omakura), a double-portion zaru soba with nine bundles — a quiet indulgence for serious soba eaters.
Soba yu (the hot cooking water) is served at the end of the meal and noted for its gentle, clean flavor — a Togakushi tradition.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Udon (100% wheat) is prepared and served in the same 11-seat counter kitchen alongside soba. Cross-contamination with wheat flour is highly probable. No separation noted in any source.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Dipping tsuyu is described as sharp and soy-forward, consistent with standard wheat-containing soy sauce. No tamari or GF soy sauce alternative is mentioned in any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: All broth is katsuobushi (bonito) based. No kombu-only or vegan dashi option is noted. Animal-stock contact is unavoidable.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Solo elderly owner; no allergy handling, allergen menu, or dietary accommodation noted in any source. No English communication indicated. Contact at 026-234-2670 if needed.

positives.text: Buckwheat soba itself is a naturally gluten-free grain; the noodles are hand-cut by the owner from Togakushi-sourced flour — no industrial additives noted.

caveats: Hours conflict between sources: Tabelog English lists 17:00-21:00 dinner close; Gondo shopping guide lists 17:00-22:00. The 21:00 close is corroborated by the 2025 blog and HotPepper; used 21:00. Dinner closing could vary — call ahead (026-234-2670).

lead: SOBA＆DINER 戸隠つきや 門前
name_ja: SOBA＆DINER 戸隠つきや 門前
cuisine: 戸隠そば・一品料理・地酒
address_ja: 長野県長野市長野桜枝町916-1
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 長野県長野市大字長野桜枝町９１６番地; 362m from Zenkoji reference point, well within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 善光寺周辺
vegan_detail: Dashi broth is fish-based (鰹節 — katsuobushi), confirmed by visitor reports describing the distinctive dried-fish dashi aroma. No vegan dashi alternative is documented. Side dishes include kamo-negi soba (duck) and horse meat yukhoe. Hidden animal ingredients in the broth make this unsafe for vegans without direct kitchen negotiation.
gf_detail: Standard Togakure soba in Japan typically uses a blend of buckwheat and wheat flour (つなぎ) unless explicitly labeled 十割 (100% buckwheat). This restaurant's soba type (buckwheat-to-wheat ratio) is not documented on any fetched source. Tsuyu (dipping sauce) contains soy sauce, which in Japan standardly contains wheat. Tempura is on the menu, representing a significant cross-contamination risk. No gluten-free menu, dedicated preparation area, or allergy protocol has been documented. Guests with celiac disease must inquire directly (Japanese only).
closed_days: 月曜・火曜（不定休あり）
seats_text: カウンター席含む約24席。一人客も入りやすい設計。
cultural_comfort_note: No English menu or signage documented; staff communication is expected to be in Japanese only.

bio.background: Opened August 2, 2024 as the Nagano sister location of a Tokyo izakaya-soba concept. The owners have roots in the Togakure area of Nagano and previously ran an izakaya in Nagano City before establishing a Nishi-Azabu outpost in Tokyo.
bio.specialty: 戸隠霧下そば（Togakure kiri-shita soba）— buckwheat grown at high altitude in the Togakure highlands where temperature swings and persistent fog concentrate flavor. Served in the traditional ぼっち盛り style: five compact bundles arranged on a bamboo sieve, echoing the five deities of the Ama-no-Iwato legend tied to Togakure.
bio.philosophy: Seasonal and local: the kitchen uses wild mountain vegetables the owner gathers personally in spring, and foraged mushrooms in autumn. The result is a soba-izakaya that pairs aromatic buckwheat noodles with carefully chosen Shinshu sake.

anecdotes.text: The soba dipping broth at つきや is notably different from the sweeter Nagano norm — reviewers describe a pronounced katsuobushi aroma that leans toward a Tokyo-style tsuyu, reflecting the owners' years running a soba izakaya in Nishi-Azabu.

The dining room fuses Showa retro warmth with a café-bar aesthetic — counter seats make solo diners comfortable, and daytime drinking is welcomed, making it unusual among soba shops in the monzen district.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Tempura is a menu staple here (かき揚げ丼, 天丼, 天ぷら盛). Fryer oil is almost certainly shared; no dedicated GF fryer is documented.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Tsuyu (dipping broth) and likely all cooking sauces use standard Japanese soy sauce, which contains wheat. No wheat-free soy sauce alternative is mentioned on any source.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Fish-based dashi (katsuobushi) is used in the tsuyu and broth. Duck (鴨ねぎ) and horse meat (ユッケ) appear on the menu. No plant-based dashi alternative documented.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy menu, allergy questionnaire, or documented staff allergy protocol found across any source. Phone reservations are accepted (026-233-8028); celiac or vegan diners should call ahead in Japanese to discuss needs.

positives.text: Buckwheat (soba) is naturally gluten-free in its pure form. If the shop uses 十割そば (100% buckwheat, no wheat binder), the noodles themselves could be GF — but this has not been confirmed for this restaurant.

caveats: Hours confirmed from Tabelog and official site as Wed-Sun split shift; irregular closures noted. Seat count varies by source (24 per official site, 25 per Web-Komachi) — 24 used. The soba wheat-binder ratio (十割 vs. mixed) is unconfirmed and is the critical unknown for GF diners. No English-language sources found; all dietary safety conclusions inferred from standard Japanese soba-restaurant practices, not from direct restaurant disclosure.

cuisine: Japanese kaiseki / kaiseki-ryori
address_ja: 〒380-0831 長野県長野市大字長野字東町125 ぱてぃお大門 蔵楽庭内 養気館二階
geocode_note: Geocoded via GSI AddressSearch on 長野県長野市大字長野字東町125; coordinates resolve to Higashimachi within Patio Daimon complex, approximately 0.69 km from Zenkoji — well within the 1.61 km target radius.
neighborhood: Zenkoji approach / Daimon-cho (東町, Patio Daimon complex)
gf_detail: Traditional kaiseki cuisine routinely uses wheat-containing soy sauce (shoyu) in marinades, sauces, and simmered dishes. Dashi is typically made with bonito (katsuo) and/or kombu; flour-based preparations may appear in steamed dishes. No gluten-free menu or dedicated allergen protocol is published on the official site or in any review. Because all dining is reservation-only, guests with celiac disease or wheat allergy should contact the restaurant in advance (026-233-7600) to discuss whether substitutions are possible — there is no indication this has been done before.
vegan_detail: Kaiseki courses are built around seasonal fish, seafood, and Nagano branded meats; dashi is bonito-based. No vegan or plant-based menu exists. Shinshu traditional vegetables are featured as accompaniments, not as the core. Reservation-only format may allow advance requests for plant-based adjustments, but no evidence of this has been published.
seats_text: 24 seats across private tatami rooms with table-and-chair seating; a separate hanare (detached cottage) kura is available for private bookings.
hours_raw: Lunch 11:30–15:00, Dinner 17:30–22:30; reservations required for both sessions; irregular closed days (不定休)
closed_days: Irregular (不定休) — no fixed weekly closure; verify before visiting
cultural_comfort_note: Reservation-only kaiseki with private tatami rooms and formal Japanese hospitality; English menus and some English-speaking staff are reportedly available, but the experience is rooted in traditional Japanese fine-dining etiquette.
caveats: Closed days are irregular; always confirm by phone before visiting. GF and vegan feasibility is unconfirmed — advance telephone inquiry is essential. A 10% service charge is added to all courses. Cancellations made on the day of dining are charged at the full rate.

bio.background: Chef Nakamura was born in Nagano Prefecture in 1976 and trained at Japanese restaurants across Nagano and Tokyo before returning to his home prefecture. He opened Shunka in 2005 and relocated it to the historic Yokikan building within the Patio Daimon complex in 2014, simultaneously opening the sister restaurant Tempura Kakure Shunka on the first floor.
bio.origin: Nagano Prefecture, Japan
bio.specialty: Seasonal kaiseki courses featuring Shinshu heirloom vegetables and Nagano branded meats; the chef's signature approach reduces the traditional proliferation of small kaiseki courses in favour of fewer, more substantial and impactful dishes
bio.philosophy: Nakamura's guiding principle is to convey 'the everlasting taste of Shinshu through kaiseki as the seasons change.' He has reinvented the conventional kaiseki format — replacing many small bites with fewer generous courses — and has studied the tea ceremony to deepen his understanding of Japanese hospitality and aesthetics.

anecdotes.text: When the original Shunka location suffered fire damage, Nakamura moved operations to an adjacent annex on the second floor of the Meiji-era Yokikan pavilion — a 150-year-old former reception hall within Patio Daimon — which has since become the permanent and atmospheric home of the restaurant.

Nakamura offers a dish he calls 'Chef's Ohagi' — rice cooked in dashi made from local Kijima mochi rice blended with Koshihikari — as a reflection of his belief that even the most humble preparation deserves technique and regional identity.

gf_cross_contamination.text: No information about cross-contamination controls has been published. The kitchen prepares traditional kaiseki where wheat-based soy sauce is a foundational ingredient; the risk of cross-contact for celiac diners is unquantified.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Standard Japanese soy sauce (shoyu), which contains wheat, is a core ingredient in kaiseki cooking — used in marinades, simmered preparations (nimono), and dipping sauces. No wheat-free tamari or alternative is mentioned anywhere on the official site or in any review.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Bonito-based dashi (katsuo dashi) is the expected stock base for kaiseki cuisine. No vegan dashi alternative is mentioned. Seafood, fish, poultry, and beef all appear across the seasonal menus.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The Hitosara listing notes that 'allergen accommodations are available upon request,' but no protocol or specific substitutions are described. The reservation-only format does allow for advance dietary communication by phone.

positives.text: All dining is by advance reservation only, creating a natural window for guests to communicate dietary needs before arrival — an advantage over walk-in venues.
English menus and reportedly some English-speaking staff are available, reducing communication barriers for foreign diners with dietary needs.

cuisine: Natural sourdough bakery
address_ja: 長野県長野市大字長野花咲町811-1
geocode_note: GSI geocode for 長野県長野市大字長野花咲町811-1 → [138.184311, 36.659935]. ~0.3km from Zenko-ji target point, well within 1.61km radius.
neighborhood: 花咲町（善光寺下駅近く）
vegan_detail: Vegan croissants (バター・牛乳・卵不使用) are a confirmed menu item. One source indicates core hard breads also use no dairy or eggs. However the bakery uses wheat flour throughout — this is not a vegan-dedicated shop and the menu changes daily/seasonally. Always confirm on the day.
gf_detail: This is a wheat bakery. All breads are made with Nagano-grown wheat flour, whole wheat, and rye. No gluten-free products are mentioned on any source. Cross-contamination from wheat flour is unavoidable throughout the kitchen.
hours_raw: 木・金・土 9:30〜17:30（売切次第閉店）、月・火・水・日定休
closed_days: 月・火・水・日曜定休
seats_text: Takeaway-focused bakery in a small renovated house; no dedicated dining seating.
cultural_comfort_note: A quiet residential-area bakery with no apparent English signage or staff; pointing and basic Japanese numbers will get you through, but no English menu or guidance should be expected.

bio.background: Pinatis was opened in June 2019 by a baker who relocated from Tokyo to Nagano's historic Zenko-ji gate district. The owner personally searched the neighborhood on foot for the right property, settling on a renovated old house in the quiet residential enclave of Hanasaki-cho. The shop runs just three days a week — Thursday through Saturday — with the baker working through the night listening to the radio to have fresh loaves ready by 9:30 AM.
bio.specialty: Hard-style sourdough loaves — particularly pain de campagne — made with Nagano-grown wheat, whole wheat, and rye flours, leavened with wild yeast cultivated from seasonal local fruits (kinkan, strawberries from the shop's own garden, Iizuna apples, persimmons, prunes).
bio.philosophy: No added sugar or oil in the core breads; ingredient-forward simplicity. The yeast culture rotates with the seasons, giving the bread a subtly different character throughout the year. Additives are avoided entirely.
bio.origin: Tokyo (transplant to Nagano)

anecdotes.text: The baker finds bread-making meditative — describing the late-night baking sessions before opening day as half relaxation, working by radio light while the dough ferments.
The vegan croissant — made without butter, milk, or eggs — appeared on the menu as a permanent item, an unusual offering for a hard-bread-focused sourdough bakery.

gf_cross_contamination.text: This is an active wheat bakery. All products are made with wheat, whole wheat, and rye flours. Flour dust and cross-contamination are inherent to the environment. Not suitable for celiac disease or serious gluten intolerance.

vegan_cross_contact.text: The kitchen handles dairy and egg ingredients (standard croissants alongside vegan croissants). Cross-contact risk for strict vegans cannot be ruled out without direct confirmation from the baker.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergy-handling protocols are documented in any source. The shop is a very small one-person operation; contact via phone (026-405-4769), Instagram, or LINE before visiting if allergies are a concern.

positives.text: Core breads contain no added sugar, oil, or additives. Vegan croissants (no butter, milk, or eggs) are a confirmed menu item.
All ingredients are domestic; no artificial additives or preservatives used.

caveats: Hours listed as Thu-Sat 9:30-17:30 but the shop frequently sells out well before closing — arriving early is strongly advised. Menu changes daily and seasonally; vegan croissants may not always be available. Check Instagram (@pinatis_baker) or LINE before visiting. PayPay QR accepted in addition to cash.

name_ja: お昼ごはんとおやつ カフェ日々
name_en: Ohiru Gohan to Oyatsu Cafe Hibi
cuisine: カフェ・スイーツ（手作りパン、季節のプレート、焼き菓子）
address_ja: 長野県長野市諏訪町509
geocode_note: GSI geocoder returned chō-block level precision for 諏訪町 (no street-number resolution). Coordinates place the spot at approx 36.6485, 138.1948 — within the target bounding box and approximately 1.59 km from Zenkoji, just inside the 1.61 km radius. Treat as approximate; street-number precision not available from GSI for this address.
neighborhood: 権堂 / 善光寺門前 (諏訪町, 市立図書館そば)
vegan_detail: The menu is built around homemade bread (domestic wheat, Hoshino natural yeast), custard pudding (eggs and dairy), chiffon cake, and seasonal curry with chicken. No vegan dishes are offered or claimed. Hidden animal-derived ingredients are routine throughout (butter, eggs, dairy in sweets; chicken in curry). Not suitable for vegans.
gf_detail: The café's core identity is homemade bread made with domestic wheat flour and Hoshino natural yeast — gluten is central to the menu, not avoided. No gluten-free options are listed or claimed anywhere. The kitchen handles wheat daily. Cross-contamination is a near certainty. Not suitable for celiac or gluten-intolerant diners.
seats_text: Roughly 10 seats in a very small, intimate space; entirely counter and small tables. The blog reviewer noted 6–7 person capacity, Tabelog lists ~10. Expect a wait or book ahead.
closed_days: 日・月・火・金・祝
cultural_comfort_note: A solo-run, very small neighborhood cafe with no English signage or menu; reservations are made via Instagram DM in Japanese, and walk-ins during busy periods may be turned away without explanation.

bio.background: A one-person operation run by its owner Rinco near the Zenkoji temple gate in Nagano's Suwacho alley district, beside the city library. The cafe celebrated its 10th anniversary around 2021, suggesting it opened circa 2011.
bio.specialty: Handmade bread plate (自家製パンプレート) — sourdough baked fresh each morning using domestic wheat and Hoshino natural yeast, served with a seasonal accompaniment. Custard pudding (昔ながらのカスタードプリン) is the signature sweet.
bio.philosophy: Wholesome, body-friendly ingredients — unrefined kibi sugar, organic tea, natural leavening — served in a quiet, carefully curated space where 'each decorative piece reflects refined taste.' The menu rotates weekly with the seasons.

anecdotes.text: A reviewer noted the cafe holds only 6–7 people and turned away walk-ins during her visit; she had reserved via Instagram DM and described it as a 'shabby chic space' where 'time flows leisurely' and the customer base was entirely female.
The blue entrance door and pale blue/white interior walls with woody textures give the tiny alley cafe an immediately recognizable character on the back street near Zenkoji.

gf_cross_contamination.text: Wheat flour is handled daily throughout the kitchen — the café's signature product is freshly baked bread. No GF protocols are mentioned anywhere.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Eggs, butter, and dairy are used throughout the menu in cakes, pudding, and bread. No vegan separation is practiced or claimed.

staff_allergy_handling.text: No allergen handling policy is published on the official site or referenced in any review. Given the one-person kitchen, allergy accommodation is unlikely without direct advance inquiry.

positives.text: Uses unrefined kibi sugar, domestic wheat, Hoshino natural yeast, and organic/caffeine-free teas — a commitment to clean, minimally processed ingredients, though this does not address gluten or vegan concerns.

caveats: Hours are irregular — the cafe sometimes closes unexpectedly. Verify via Instagram before visiting. Reservation strongly recommended (Instagram DM or phone). GSI geocode is chō-block precision only; exact street placement should be confirmed on Google Maps. Payment: no credit cards; electronic money (IC cards etc.) accepted alongside cash. The 1.59 km distance estimate from Zenkoji is near the edge of the 1.61 km radius and based on approximate geocoding — treat as borderline.
